title: CI hardening follow-up: security-scanner install + CI-aware scan test + CodeQL continue-on-error
detail:
PR #35 (iterate-2026-05-18-fix-launch-blocker-tests) fixed the 17 launch-blocker test failures, but activating the CI workflows (launch Step 4) surfaced CI-environment issues the dormant workflows had masked.

CI run on PR #35 failed:
  1-3) plugins/shipwright-security tests/test_oss_backend_smoke.py (semgrep / gitleaks / trivy smoke tests) hard-fail in CI because ci.yml runs the security plugin's tests but never installs those scanners - only security.yml does. Per ADR-044 these tests fail-loud in CI by design.
  4) tests/test_run_scan_and_report.py::test_full_evidence_retains_raw_secret_fields fails: the scanner refuses --full-evidence when CI=true and the test does not handle that guard.

CodeQL run failed separately: 'Advanced Security must be enabled for this repository' - codeql.yml lacks the continue-on-error guard security.yml already carries for private repos without code scanning.

Fix scope: (a) ci.yml installs semgrep/gitleaks/trivy before the plugin-test loop (mirror security.yml), or skip the tool-dependent security tests in the basic CI job; (b) make test_full_evidence_retains_raw_secret_fields CI-aware; (c) add continue-on-error to codeql.yml's analyze/upload step, or enable GitHub code scanning in repo settings (resolves on going public). Decided as follow-up: user chose 'merge #35, rest as follow-up'.

title: Phase-quality SessionStart hook prints 13x — give it a single owner
detail: The SessionStart phase-quality hook is registered in every plugin's hooks.json (~13 plugins), so the same context message prints 13x per session. Follow-up: give it a single owner (shipwright-compliance) so it registers exactly once. Noted as out-of-scope in iterate-2026-05-18-phase-quality-check-fixes (PR #37).

title: write_decision_drop.py not worktree-aware — F3 decision-drops lost on worktree cleanup
detail: Since unconditional worktree isolation (ADR-049), iterate F3 writes the decision-drop into the WORKTREE's .shipwright/agent_docs/decision-drops/. That dir is gitignored by design (launch-prep plan: drops are 'Staging', aggregated into decision_log.md). But write_decision_drop.py uses project_root/.shipwright/... directly — NOT worktree-aware — so the drop lands in the worktree and is destroyed by 'git worktree remove' before /shipwright-changelog's aggregate_decisions.py can fold it into decision_log.md. Net effect: every iterate ADR since ADR-049 is silently lost from decision_log.md aggregation. Fix: make write_decision_drop.py resolve the MAIN repo via git-common-dir and write the drop there, mirroring record_event.py / resolve_events_path (ADR-050). The gitignore decision stays unchanged. Identified during iterate-2026-05-18-phase-quality-check-fixes (PR #37).

title: SBOM cluster: non-determinism + broken cleanup launch-payloads
detail: Two real bugs in SBOM subsystem (ADR-056) surfaced during 2026-05-22 bloat-cleanup follow-up. (1) Non-determinism: consecutive sbom.md regenerations produce structurally different output — 'Unique licenses: 3 (Apache-2.0, MIT, unknown)' vs 'Unique licenses: 2 (MIT, unknown)' for the same monorepo. License resolver appears to read venv state non-reproducibly. (2) Broken cleanup launch-payload: the auto-emitted hint 'cd plugins/<x> && uv sync && update_compliance' for trg-95fba1e3 (requests) and trg-db49aad2 (google-genai) does NOT clear the items — uv sync resolves dep graph but doesn't add license metadata that the SBOM resolver picks up; packages stay 'unknown'. Either the resolver is broken (should read PKG-INFO/METADATA from site-packages directly) or the launch payload is misleading. Fix needed analogous to d325fd6 (deterministic-render-timestamps): pin SBOM resolution to uv.lock or installed METADATA, NOT live venv probe. Two follow-up iterates needed: (A) SBOM determinism, (B) SBOM license-resolver actually reads PKG-INFO.

detail: Bundle SP2 + SP4. title: [P5.1] WebUI Quick Wins: optimistic mutations + self-hosting tier docs
detail: Bundle MU4 + MU6. (a) Triage actions (Promote/Dismiss/Snooze/FixNow) get TanStack-Query useMutation with onMutate/onError/onSettled - eliminates visible roundtrip latency. (b) Both repos split docs into README quickstart + docs/guide.md daily + new SELF_HOSTING.md ops. Required for public launch. Effort: 1 iterate (S). Sequencing: parallel-able with P3-P4. title: [P6.1] WebUI: WebSocket streaming for iterate progress
detail: MU1: New /api/ws/iterate/<projectId> endpoint that streams events.jsonl appends to TaskDetailPage. Client subscribes on TaskDetail mount; replaces 1s polling for new events (initial load stays REST). WS disconnect -> fallback to polling, no hard-failure. Touches: shipwright-webui server/src/external/ + client TaskDetailPage. Effort: 1 iterate (M-L, ~2 days). Sequencing: mid-term, after P5.1. Pattern from: multica-ai/multica.

title: [P6.2] WebUI: Multi-Workspace isolation
detail: MU2: Workspace layer in ~/.shipwright-webui/workspaces.json. Sidebar workspace switcher. React-Query keys prefixed with wsId -> automatic cache isolation on switch. Migration: existing projects land in workspace 'default'. Touches: shipwright-webui server + client + sidebar. Effort: 1 iterate (L, ~2-3 days). Sequencing: when 3+ projects are concurrently active. Pattern from: multica-ai/multica.

title: [P6.3] WebUI: Runtime Registry (multi-CLI: Claude/Codex/Gemini)
detail: MU3: New server/src/core/runtime-registry.ts. Each Runtime adapter declares: launch-command-template, transcript-discovery-pattern, session-id argument, supported *_config.json. First iterate extracts Claude-adapter + Codex-adapter. Adding Gemini becomes single-file-add. TaskDetailPage gets Runtime dropdown. Effort: 1 iterate (M-L, ~2 days). Sequencing: when Codex/Gemini support becomes a serious goal. Pattern from: multica-ai/multica.

title: [P7.1] Plugin Boundary Hardening: import-boundary check + Pydantic configs
detail: Bundle MU-PL2 + MU-PL3. (a) New scripts/check_plugin_boundaries.py: AST-scan that plugins/shipwright-X/scripts/ never imports from plugins/shipwright-Y/scripts/; shared/scripts/ may be imported by all. Integrated in pre-commit + CI. Analog to existing check_plugin_cache_sync.py. (b) Pydantic models per shipwright_*_config.json schema in shared/scripts/lib/config_schemas.py; readers validate via model_validate_json, warn once per session on ValidationError. Effort: 1 iterate (M). Sequencing: when plugin-cross-talk or config-drift becomes a real footgun. Patterns from: multica-ai/multica.

title: [P8.1] Architecture Research Sprint: internal-packages + daemon + skills-lock
detail: Bundle MU5 + MU7 + MU-PL1. Single research iterate producing three decision documents (or ADR-stubs): (a) WebUI: does extracting client+server shared types into packages/shared/ justify a pnpm/npm-workspace monorepo? (b) WebUI: do background workers (file watchers, polling) need to move into a separate daemon, or stay in the Hono process? (c) Shipwright: does Claude Code's native extraKnownMarketplaces cover third-party Shipwright phases, or do we need an additional hash-pinned skills-lock.json? Effort: 1 research iterate (M-L). Sequencing: when planning the next architectural milestone. Patterns from: multica-ai/multica.

title: [P1.1] Public-Launch Hardening: Anti-Slop PR template + Karpathy in constitution
detail:
## Intent

Bundle P1.1 from Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md (SP5 + KA1). Public-Launch Hardening — small but blocker.

## Files

- shared/constitution.md (header insert; stays <=400 LOC)
- .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md (full rewrite)
- C:/01_Development/shipwright-webui/.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md (NEW)
- README.md (footer expand)
- docs/guide.md (extend 'Integrated Learnings', Z. 136 ff.)
- C:/01_Development/shipwright-webui/README.md (new Acknowledgments block before License)

## Acceptance

- Constitution.md adds a Pre-Phase Principles header with the 4 Karpathy principles cited verbatim + MIT attribution to multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills (2026-05-25 snapshot date).
- PR template adopts Superpowers anti-slop framing in both repos; MIT attribution footer to obra/superpowers.
- README + guide acknowledgments cover Superpowers / Osmani / Karpathy / Multica with per-source attribution; Multica explicitly 'Apache-2.0 modified, patterns only, no code copy'.
- No bloat-gate trip; baseline update if constitution.md grows past 400 LOC.

## External Sources (MIT, attribute in files)

- obra/superpowers — anti-slop PR-template framing
- multica-ai/andrej-karpathy-skills — 4 Karpathy principles

## Spec Reference

Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md sections: 6 (P1.1 bundle), 8 (patch text), 1.1 (boundary with Campaign A.defense — constitution.md section 21 already updated by A.defense, this adds a separate Pre-Phase Principles header).

## Fix-Now Hint

Two repos in scope — open two PRs, one per repo, cross-link in PR descriptions.

title: [P3.1] Reviewer Stack: spec-reviewer + doubt-reviewer subagents
detail:
## Intent

Bundle P3.1 from Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md (SP1 + OS3). Add two new reviewer subagents alongside the existing code-reviewer. Build skill orchestrates them in sequence: spec-reviewer (compliance) -> code-reviewer (quality) -> doubt-reviewer (only for non-trivial decisions: migrations, async/concurrency, cross-plugin imports, irreversible ops).

## Files

- plugins/shipwright-build/agents/spec-reviewer.md (NEW, ~150 LOC, adversarial spec-compliance prompt with HARD-GATE tag in Superpowers style)
- plugins/shipwright-build/agents/doubt-reviewer.md (NEW, ~150 LOC, fresh-context adversarial prompt biased to disprove per Osmani doubt-driven)
- plugins/shipwright-build/skills/build/SKILL.md (orchestration: spec-reviewer -> code-reviewer -> conditional doubt-reviewer based on file-touch heuristic)
- plugins/shipwright-build/tests/test_reviewer_orchestration.py (NEW)

## Acceptance

- Probe-build with deliberately spec-divergent code: spec-reviewer rejects with explicit spec-line citation; code-reviewer not invoked yet.
- Probe-build touching supabase/migrations/*.sql: doubt-reviewer invoked AFTER code-reviewer passes.
- Probe-build touching only README.md: doubt-reviewer NOT invoked (trivial change heuristic).
- No bloat trip on agents/*.md (each <=400 LOC).
- All three reviewers carry MIT attribution footer.

## External Sources (MIT, attribute in files)

- obra/superpowers — subagent-driven-development two-stage review pattern (spec-reviewer)
- addyosmani/agent-skills — doubt-driven-development fresh-context adversarial pattern (doubt-reviewer)

## Spec Reference

Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md sections: 2 (SP1 + OS3 spec-items), 6 (P3.1 bundle), 1.1 (boundary with Campaign A.review — A.review only added Bloat-Checklist to EXISTING code-reviewer.md and sub-iterate-runner.md; this iterate adds NEW reviewer agents).

## Fix-Now Hint

Shipwright-monorepo iterate. One PR.

title: [P3.2] Code-Simplify Skill (standalone, behavior-preserving)
detail:
## Intent

P3.2 (OS1) from Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md. Standalone Code-Simplify Skill that runs behavior-preserving refactors. Five Osmani principles (Preserve Behavior, Follow Conventions, Clarity over Cleverness, Maintain Balance, Scope to What Changed) + Chesterton-Fence check + behavior-snapshot test (run full test suite before+after, reject if behavior diff).

## Files

Decision in iterate (choose ONE):
(a) Sub-skill: plugins/shipwright-iterate/skills/iterate/references/F-simplify.md (NEW) — triggers when iterate intent is 'simplify X' / 'clean up Y' / 'refactor Z'.
(b) Mini-plugin: plugins/shipwright-simplify/ (NEW directory with full plugin scaffolding) — exposed as /shipwright-simplify slash command.

Recommend (a) for speed; (b) only if /shipwright-simplify should be discoverable as its own command.

## Acceptance

- /shipwright-iterate 'simplify the helper in X' triggers Behavior-Snapshot (run test suite, store green-state hash).
- Edit phase applies five-principle check + Chesterton-Fence pre-flight ('before deleting/changing, can you state WHY this exists?').
- Behavior-Verify re-runs test suite; reject if any test status flips OR if line-count drop accompanied by removed test coverage.
- Probe-iterate: simplify a known-fixable function -> green; simplify a function with hidden side effect -> rejected.

## External Sources (MIT, attribute in files)

- addyosmani/agent-skills — code-simplification skill (Five Principles, Chesterton-Fence, 'fewer lines is not the goal')

## Spec Reference

Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md sections: 2 (OS1 spec-item), 6 (P3.2 bundle).

## Fix-Now Hint

Shipwright-monorepo iterate. One PR.

title: [P5.1] WebUI Quick Wins: optimistic mutations + self-hosting tier docs
detail:

title: [P6.1] WebUI: WebSocket streaming for iterate progress
detail:

## Files

WebUI repo (C:/01_Development/shipwright-webui):
- server/src/external/routes.ts (add WS handler) — NOTE: this file is target of Track-C C2 cleanup (split into sub-routers). If C2 hasn't run yet, coordinate: either land MU1 first (one more file in the to-split set), or wait for C2 to land and add MU1 to one of its sub-routers (cleaner).
- server/src/external/ws-iterate.ts (NEW, only if C2 ordering allows post-split)
- client/src/hooks/useIterateEventsStream.ts (NEW, ~80 LOC)

- client/src/lib/eventsApi.ts (extend with WS variant)

title: [P6.2] WebUI: Multi-Workspace isolation
detail:
## Intent

P6.2 (MU2) from Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md. Multi-Workspace layer: organize Shipwright projects under named workspaces (e.g. 'customer-A', 'oss'). Workspace-scoped React-Query keys give automatic cache isolation on switch. Existing projects migrate to a 'default' workspace.

## Files

WebUI repo (C:/01_Development/shipwright-webui):
- server/src/core/workspace-store.ts (NEW; reads/writes ~/.shipwright-webui/workspaces.json with proper-lockfile)
- server/src/routes/workspaces.ts (NEW; CRUD for workspaces)
- server/src/types/workspace.ts (NEW; Workspace + WorkspaceId types)
- client/src/components/sidebar/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx (NEW; dropdown in sidebar)
- client/src/lib/workspaceContext.ts (NEW; ZustState store for active wsId)
- client/src/lib/projectsApi.ts (extend with wsId param)
- client/src/hooks/useProjects.ts (key on [wsId, 'projects'])
- Migration: ~/.shipwright-webui/migrate-to-workspaces.ts (NEW; runs once on startup if workspaces.json absent)

## Acceptance

- Two workspaces ('default' + 'customer-A') each with 3 projects.
- Switcher in sidebar changes active workspace; sidebar project list + Triage counts re-render correctly.
- React-Query cache keyed on [wsId, ...] -> switching workspaces shows correct data instantly (no flash of wrong content).
- Cross-workspace navigation: clicking a project in workspace B from workspace A switches workspace AND opens the project (single click).
- Migration runs once: existing ~/.shipwright-webui/projects.json -> workspace 'default' contains all projects.

## External Sources (patterns only, no code copy)

- multica-ai/multica — workspace-scoped queries pattern (wsId in cache key, automatic isolation)

Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md sections: 2 (MU2 spec-item), 6 (P6.2 bundle).

## Fix-Now Hint

WebUI repo iterate. One PR. ~2-3 days. Migration safety: backup ~/.shipwright-webui/ before first run.

title: [P7.1] Plugin Boundary Hardening: import-boundary + Pydantic configs
detail:
## Intent

Bundle P7.1 from Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md (MU-PL2 + MU-PL3). Two hardening patterns: (a) AST-based plugin import-boundary check so plugins/shipwright-X/scripts/ cannot accidentally depend on plugins/shipwright-Y/scripts/; (b) Pydantic schemas for shipwright_*_config.json so plugins reading each other's configs survive schema drift.

## Files

Shipwright repo:
- scripts/check_plugin_boundaries.py (NEW, <=300 LOC; AST scan, analog to existing scripts/check_plugin_cache_sync.py)
- shared/scripts/lib/config_schemas.py (NEW; Pydantic models for shipwright_run_config.json, shipwright_iterate_config.json, etc.)
- plugins/*/scripts/lib/config_reader.py wherever it exists (migrate to use model_validate_json; warn-once-then-fallback)
- .github/workflows/boundary-check.yml (NEW; runs on PR)
- scripts/hooks/pre-commit (extend to call check_plugin_boundaries.py)
- shared/tests/test_check_plugin_boundaries.py (NEW)
- shared/tests/test_config_schemas.py (NEW)

## Acceptance

- Probe-edit: add 'from plugins.shipwright_test.scripts.lib.foo import bar' to plugins/shipwright-build/scripts/x.py -> pre-commit fails with clear violation message.
- Allowed: any plugin importing from shared/scripts/.
- Probe-edit: write malformed shipwright_run_config.json -> reader warns once to stderr, falls back to defaults, does NOT crash.
- Schema drift (extra field): silently accepted (forward-compat).
- Schema drift (missing required field): warn-once + fallback default.

## External Sources (patterns only, no code copy)

- multica-ai/multica — hard-package-boundary rules ('packages/core zero react-dom') applied to Python plugin imports
- multica-ai/multica — API Response Compatibility 'parse, don't cast' applied to inter-plugin JSON configs

## Spec Reference

Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md sections: 2 (MU-PL2 + MU-PL3 spec-items), 6 (P7.1 bundle).

## Fix-Now Hint

Shipwright-monorepo iterate. One PR. ~1.5 days. Run after at least one B-cleanup iterate so the AST scan sees the refactored plugin structure.

title: [P8.1] Architecture Research Sprint: internal-packages + daemon + skills-lock
detail:
## Intent

Bundle P8.1 from Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md (MU5 + MU7 + MU-PL1). Single research iterate producing three decision documents (ADR-stubs). No production code in this iterate — output is read-and-decide artifacts.

## Files

Three new ADR stubs under .shipwright/planning/adr/research/ (NEW dir):
- ADR-RESEARCH-monorepo-internal-packages.md
  Context: WebUI duplicates types across client/ and server/. Should we adopt a pnpm/npm-workspace monorepo with packages/shared/?
  Decision (output): needed / not-needed + migration cost.
- ADR-RESEARCH-webui-daemon-pattern.md
  Context: WebUI's background workers (file watchers, polling) run in the Hono process. Should they move to a separate daemon?
  Decision (output): needed / not-needed + threshold conditions (e.g. 'when X concurrent projects exceed Y').
- ADR-RESEARCH-shipwright-skills-lock.md
  Context: Multica uses skills-lock.json with hash-pinned external skill sources. Claude Code has native extraKnownMarketplaces — is an additional lock layer worth it for third-party Shipwright phases?
  Decision (output): needed / not-needed + complementary-or-replacement assessment.

## Acceptance

- Three ADR stubs filled with Context / Options / Decision / Consequences / Re-Review-Date.
- Each carries a 1-line summary at the top so a busy reader sees the verdict immediately.
- Decision is explicit (not 'TBD' / 'maybe later'); even 'not needed for now' counts as a decision with a re-review trigger.
- All three reference the Multica patterns they evaluate, with Apache-2.0-modified disclaimer.

## External Sources (patterns only, no code copy)

- multica-ai/multica — internal-packages, daemon, skills-lock patterns to evaluate

## Spec Reference

Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md sections: 2 (MU5 + MU7 + MU-PL1 spec-items), 6 (P8.1 bundle).

## Fix-Now Hint

Shipwright-monorepo iterate. One PR. Research-only -- no code, no tests. Output is three decision docs. ~1-1.5 days reading + writing.

cd 'plugins/shipwright-compliance' \

reason: Realized via manual /shipwright-iterate (not WebUI Fix-Now flow). Work shipped as shipwright PR #105 + shipwright-webui PR #72 (both open, cross-linked). No automated triage flow needed.

title: Dirty main tree after Stop: untrack runtime artifacts (B) + sandbox iterate_stop_finalize repair pass
detail: Two bugs cause the recurring 'main is dirty after every session' pattern (verified against session 40b1eb76 end-state, 8 dirty files in main, 49 Stop hooks fired in last turn). SCOPE 1 (Option B from analysis 2026-05-25): Stop hook regenerates session_handoff.md + build_dashboard.md + triage_inbox.md every turn, all 3 tracked -> dirty after every Stop, merge conflicts on every long-lived branch. Fix: move writes to .shipwright/agent_docs/runtime/ (gitignored), iterate-finalize snapshots into tracked paths (same single-producer pattern as PR #78 for compliance MDs). SCOPE 2 (newly discovered via verification): iterate_stop_finalize.py repair pass runs finalize_iterate against main tree when _active_worktree_root() returns None (stale session->worktree pointer or worktree removed post-merge). finalize writes the 5 compliance MDs in main, bypassing #78's single-producer guarantee. Fix: repair pass must refuse to run (or exit silently) when no valid worktree pointer exists for the current SHIPWRIGHT_SESSION_ID; never fall back to cwd. SCOPE 3 (Option C): codify 'git merge, not git rebase' when integrating main into iterate branches with Run-ID trailers, to preserve F5b commit-SHA pinning. Launch payload: read .shipwright/agent_docs/decision-drops/ + this triage card, then /shipwright-iterate with title 'tracked-artifacts-single-producer-and-finalize-sandbox'. Files touched estimate: ~15 (3 hook writers + finalize-snapshot step + audit_staleness coverage + .gitignore + tests + dogfood).

reason: Re-dismissed via iterate-2026-05-27 (PR #106). Commits: 54ecb17 main fix + 36375a6 verifier correction on iterate branch; F7b 4299012 + 6c3c86c on main. Phantom audit-fixture run_id from 2026-05-02 B3 probe. SessionStart Phase-Quality FAIL re-emits from stale .shipwright/compliance/skill-compliance/iterate-b3-functional-probe-b3-functional-probe.json — silencing the noise requires deleting that file (deferred; not blocked by this iterate).

title: ADR-090 bloat exception — split deferred files by 2026-08-27
detail: 6 files bumped via ADR-090 to absorb iterate-2026-05-27 (runtime/snapshot split). Primary split candidate: shared/scripts/tools/finalize_iterate.py (352 -> 475 LOC, 4 new helpers _atomic_replace/_refuse_symlink/_unlink_runtime_artifacts/_snapshot_triage_runtime). Plan: extract into shared/scripts/lib/iterate_snapshot.py (-120 LOC). Secondary: shared/tests/test_generate_handoff_on_stop.py (337 -> 402, factor double-assertion helper). Re-review date 2026-08-27.
launchPayload: /shipwright-iterate fix: split finalize_iterate.py per ADR-090 (re-review 2026-08-27). Extract _atomic_replace, _refuse_symlink, _unlink_runtime_artifacts, _snapshot_triage_runtime into shared/scripts/lib/iterate_snapshot.py. Update bloat baseline to remove the exception. Spec: .shipwright/planning/adr/090-bloat-exception-runtime-snapshot-split.md

reason: Resolved by PR #106 (iterate-2026-05-27-tracked-artifacts-single-producer-and-finalize-sandbox). All 3 scopes shipped: runtime/ split for agent-doc trio, hard-gated finalize repair pass, merge-not-rebase convention. Commits: 54ecb17 + 36375a6 on iterate branch; F7b 4299012 + 6c3c86c on main. Follow-up triage trg-74288ab0 (ADR-090 split-deferred, re-review 2026-08-27).

SBOM auto-resolved by iterate-2026-05-27-sbom-license-resolve (PR #107, merged 0469e85). plugins/shipwright-compliance/.venv now contains pytest 9.0.3 (License-Expression: MIT) + pytest-mock 3.15.1 (License: MIT). Verified locally on main.

Re-evaluated 2026-05-27 (same day as opening): both deferred splits rejected as permanent decisions, not deferred. finalize_iterate.py at 475 LOC is long code, not bloat — coherent Ousterhout deep module, every helper called, single producer, no future consumers planned. Test file's double-assertion is the single-producer contract. ADR-090 amended with permanent-exception rationale.

title: [P3.1] Reviewer Stack: spec-reviewer + doubt-reviewer subagents
detail:
## Intent

Bundle P3.1 from Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md (SP1 + OS3). Add two new reviewer subagents alongside the existing code-reviewer. Build skill orchestrates them in sequence: spec-reviewer (compliance) -> code-reviewer (quality) -> doubt-reviewer (only for non-trivial decisions: migrations, async/concurrency, cross-plugin imports, irreversible ops).

## Files

- plugins/shipwright-build/agents/spec-reviewer.md (NEW, ~150 LOC, adversarial spec-compliance prompt with HARD-GATE tag in Superpowers style)
- plugins/shipwright-build/agents/doubt-reviewer.md (NEW, ~150 LOC, fresh-context adversarial prompt biased to disprove per Osmani doubt-driven)
- plugins/shipwright-build/skills/build/SKILL.md (orchestration: spec-reviewer -> code-reviewer -> conditional doubt-reviewer based on file-touch heuristic)
- plugins/shipwright-build/tests/test_reviewer_orchestration.py (NEW)

## Acceptance

- Probe-build with deliberately spec-divergent code: spec-reviewer rejects with explicit spec-line citation; code-reviewer not invoked yet.
- Probe-build touching supabase/migrations/*.sql: doubt-reviewer invoked AFTER code-reviewer passes.
- Probe-build touching only README.md: doubt-reviewer NOT invoked (trivial change heuristic).
- No bloat trip on agents/*.md (each <=400 LOC).
- All three reviewers carry MIT attribution footer.

## External Sources (MIT, attribute in files)

- obra/superpowers — subagent-driven-development two-stage review pattern (spec-reviewer)

detail:
## Intent

P6.2 (MU2) from Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md. Multi-Workspace layer: organize Shipwright projects under named workspaces (e.g. 'customer-A', 'oss'). Workspace-scoped React-Query keys give automatic cache isolation on switch. Existing projects migrate to a 'default' workspace.

## Files

WebUI repo (C:/01_Development/shipwright-webui):
- server/src/core/workspace-store.ts (NEW; reads/writes ~/.shipwright-webui/workspaces.json with proper-lockfile)
- server/src/routes/workspaces.ts (NEW; CRUD for workspaces)
- server/src/types/workspace.ts (NEW; Workspace + WorkspaceId types)
- client/src/components/sidebar/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx (NEW; dropdown in sidebar)
- client/src/lib/workspaceContext.ts (NEW; ZustState store for active wsId)
- client/src/lib/projectsApi.ts (extend with wsId param)
- client/src/hooks/useProjects.ts (key on [wsId, 'projects'])
- Migration: ~/.shipwright-webui/migrate-to-workspaces.ts (NEW; runs once on startup if workspaces.json absent)

## Acceptance

- Two workspaces ('default' + 'customer-A') each with 3 projects.
- Switcher in sidebar changes active workspace; sidebar project list + Triage counts re-render correctly.
- React-Query cache keyed on [wsId, ...] -> switching workspaces shows correct data instantly (no flash of wrong content).
- Cross-workspace navigation: clicking a project in workspace B from workspace A switches workspace AND opens the project (single click).
- Migration runs once: existing ~/.shipwright-webui/projects.json -> workspace 'default' contains all projects.

## External Sources (patterns only, no code copy)

- multica-ai/multica — workspace-scoped queries pattern (wsId in cache key, automatic isolation)

## Spec Reference

Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md sections: 2 (MU2 spec-item), 6 (P6.2 bundle).

## Fix-Now Hint

WebUI repo iterate. One PR. ~2-3 days. Migration safety: backup ~/.shipwright-webui/ before first run.

reason: re-prioritized to P3 (P6.2 Multi-Workspace — only at 3+ projects) as trg-41f0c3ab

title: [P6.3] WebUI: Runtime Registry (multi-CLI: Claude/Codex/Gemini)
detail:
## Intent

P6.3 (MU3) from Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md. Runtime-Adapter Registry: extract Claude + Codex launch logic into adapters declaring launch-command-template, transcript-discovery-pattern, session-id argument, and supported *_config.json. Adding a third runtime (Gemini CLI, OpenCode, etc.) becomes a single-file add.

## Files

WebUI repo (C:/01_Development/shipwright-webui):
- server/src/core/runtime-registry.ts (NEW; ~150 LOC; RuntimeAdapter interface + register/lookup)
- server/src/core/runtimes/claude-code.ts (NEW; extracted from existing launch code)
- server/src/core/runtimes/codex.ts (NEW; extracted from existing Codex-via-MCP path)
- server/src/external/routes.ts (re-route launch logic through registry — coordinate with C2 split if active)
- client/src/components/external/RuntimeDropdown.tsx (NEW; dropdown in TaskDetail header)
- client/src/components/external/LaunchPayloadBlock.tsx (extend with runtime selector)
- Tests: round-trip for each adapter (write launch-command -> spawn -> JSONL discovery)

## Acceptance

- TaskDetail header shows Runtime dropdown; Claude is default; Codex selectable.
- Switching runtime regenerates pre-bound launch command in the embedded terminal.
- JSONL discovery works for both runtimes (Claude: ~/.claude/projects/<cwd>/<uuid>.jsonl; Codex: equivalent path).
- Adding a hypothetical third adapter (e.g. echo-runtime.ts stub for testing) requires only the new file + 1-line register call.

## External Sources (patterns only, no code copy)

- multica-ai/multica — runtime-adapter pattern (11-CLI support via uniform adapter interface)

## Spec Reference

Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md sections: 2 (MU3 spec-item), 6 (P6.3 bundle).

## Fix-Now Hint

WebUI repo iterate. One PR. ~2 days. Sequence after P6.1 (WS streaming) — Codex adapter benefits from the streaming infra.

reason: re-prioritized to P3 (P6.3 Runtime-Registry — only at multi-CLI need) as trg-2b5a5a86

title: [P7.1] Plugin Boundary Hardening: import-boundary + Pydantic configs
detail:
## Intent

Bundle P7.1 from Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md (MU-PL2 + MU-PL3). Two hardening patterns: (a) AST-based plugin import-boundary check so plugins/shipwright-X/scripts/ cannot accidentally depend on plugins/shipwright-Y/scripts/; (b) Pydantic schemas for shipwright_*_config.json so plugins reading each other's configs survive schema drift.

## Files

Shipwright repo:
- scripts/check_plugin_boundaries.py (NEW, <=300 LOC; AST scan, analog to existing scripts/check_plugin_cache_sync.py)
- shared/scripts/lib/config_schemas.py (NEW; Pydantic models for shipwright_run_config.json, shipwright_iterate_config.json, etc.)
- plugins/*/scripts/lib/config_reader.py wherever it exists (migrate to use model_validate_json; warn-once-then-fallback)
- .github/workflows/boundary-check.yml (NEW; runs on PR)
- scripts/hooks/pre-commit (extend to call check_plugin_boundaries.py)
- shared/tests/test_check_plugin_boundaries.py (NEW)
- shared/tests/test_config_schemas.py (NEW)

## Acceptance

- Probe-edit: add 'from plugins.shipwright_test.scripts.lib.foo import bar' to plugins/shipwright-build/scripts/x.py -> pre-commit fails with clear violation message.
- Allowed: any plugin importing from shared/scripts/.
- Probe-edit: write malformed shipwright_run_config.json -> reader warns once to stderr, falls back to defaults, does NOT crash.
- Schema drift (extra field): silently accepted (forward-compat).
- Schema drift (missing required field): warn-once + fallback default.

## External Sources (patterns only, no code copy)

- multica-ai/multica — hard-package-boundary rules ('packages/core zero react-dom') applied to Python plugin imports
- multica-ai/multica — API Response Compatibility 'parse, don't cast' applied to inter-plugin JSON configs

## Spec Reference

Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md sections: 2 (MU-PL2 + MU-PL3 spec-items), 6 (P7.1 bundle).

## Fix-Now Hint

Shipwright-monorepo iterate. One PR. ~1.5 days. Run after at least one B-cleanup iterate so the AST scan sees the refactored plugin structure.

reason: re-prioritized to P3 (P7.1 Plugin-Boundary — only at drift-pain) as trg-dfca97e9

title: [P2.recovery] SP3 + OS2 — post-Campaign-B reintegration
detail:
## Intent

Campaign B (PRs #89-#102, merged 2026-05-26) split the iterate and
project SKILL.md files but did NOT carry the inline SP3 / OS2 patches
that Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md §6.2 prescribed as
"Bundle B2 carries SP3, B1.project carries OS2". Verified
post-merge on 2026-05-27:

- `plugins/shipwright-iterate/skills/iterate/references/F-debug.md`
  does NOT exist (F-references list goes F0/F0.5/F1/F2/F3/F3a/F4/
  F5/F5b/F5c/F6/F6.5/F7/F7b/F11/F12 — no F-debug).
- `plugins/shipwright-project/skills/project/` references contain
  NO assumptions-first / inferred-assumptions block. Grep for
  "assumptions-first|surface assumptions|inferred assumptions"
  returns zero hits in shipwright-project.

This bundle re-establishes both patches as a standalone iterate now
that Campaign B is closed and inline-mitnehmen no longer an option.

## Files

- plugins/shipwright-iterate/skills/iterate/references/F-debug.md (NEW, ≤300 LOC)
  - Iron Law (Superpowers): "NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST"
  - 4-phase structure: Read Error → Reproduce → Recent Changes → Component-Boundary Instrumentation
  - Attribution footer: Adapted from obra/superpowers (MIT, © Jesse Vincent)
- plugins/shipwright-iterate/skills/iterate/SKILL.md (PATCH)
  - Intent-classification: when kind=bug-fix → route through F-debug before F-implement
  - Reviewer gate: reject fix that addresses symptom not root cause
- plugins/shipwright-project/skills/project/references/interview-protocol.md (PATCH)
  - Pre-Phase block: "Before asking clarifying questions, list inferred
    assumptions explicitly (web-app vs CLI, stack, persistence,
    auth model) and ask for correction."
  - Attribution footer: Adapted from addyosmani/agent-skills (MIT, © Addy Osmani)
- plugins/shipwright-project/skills/project/SKILL.md (small PATCH if interview-protocol.md is loaded via reference token)
- Tests:
  - plugins/shipwright-iterate/tests/test_f_debug_routing.py (NEW)
  - plugins/shipwright-project/tests/test_assumptions_first_block.py (NEW)

## Acceptance

- Probe-iterate "fix bug: button not clickable" → routes through F-debug;
  reviewer rejects a fix that doesn't show root-cause investigation steps.
- Probe-project `/shipwright-project "build a todo app"` → first agent
  response lists inferred assumptions (e.g. "single-user, web-app,
  Postgres, session-cookies") BEFORE the first clarifying question.
- Both new files carry MIT attribution footers to their respective sources.
- No bloat trip: F-debug.md ≤400 LOC; interview-protocol.md patch ≤80 LOC delta.

## External Sources (MIT, attribute in files)

- obra/superpowers — skills/systematic-debugging/SKILL.md (4-phase + Iron Law)
- addyosmani/agent-skills — skills/spec-driven-development/SKILL.md ("Surface assumptions immediately" block)

## Spec Reference

Spec/external-frameworks-integration.md §SP3 + §OS2 + §6.2 + §10
(post-mortem: §6.2 "ride inline with Campaign B" path missed because
Campaign B sub-iterate plans did not reference SP3/OS2; this bundle
re-establishes both, see also session 3c6bc3e9-5989-4627-9874-b4c34affb3b7).

## Fix-Now Hint

/shipwright-iterate --type feature "[trg-XXX] SP3+OS2 post-Campaign-B
reintegration — F-debug.md + assumptions-first block per Spec/external-
frameworks-integration.md §6.2"

title: finalize_iterate work_completed event lands uncommitted in main tree during worktree iterates (never enters the PR)
detail:
## Summary

During a **worktree-based iterate** (the default since `setup_iterate_worktree.py`),
the `work_completed` event that `finalize_iterate.py` (F5b) records into
`shipwright_events.jsonl` lands **only as an uncommitted line in the MAIN
tree's working copy**. It is in no commit, never enters the iterate PR, and
requires a separate manual `chore(events): record evt-... for iterate-...`
commit (F7) to persist. F7 is documented as "skip unless replaying", so it is
routinely skipped -> the canonical event log silently misses entries and
`shipwright_events.jsonl` shows as dirty (`M`) at the start of essentially
every session.

This is an audit-log / compliance-integrity bug: `traceability-matrix.md`,
`change-history.md`, and the build dashboard all derive from the event log,
so a missing `work_completed` event means the iterate is invisible to those
artifacts unless someone notices the dirty file and hand-commits it.

## Symptom

- `git status` in the consuming project's main tree perpetually shows
  `M shipwright_events.jsonl`.
- The latest iterate's `work_completed` event is present in the working copy
  but absent from `HEAD:shipwright_events.jsonl`.
- A recurring stream of `chore(events): record evt-XXXX for iterate-YYY`
  commits exists purely to backfill these (8+ in shipwright-webui history:
  68a8965, f88a4e8, 5ff5c3e, 2159acd, bb37b4f, 0d1702a, ...).

## Root cause (3-way file mismatch)

Reproduced in shipwright-webui iterate-2026-05-27-transcript-renderer-scroll.
Line counts of `shipwright_events.jsonl` across the lineage:

| Ref | lines | last event id | has the new evt? |
|-----|-------|---------------|------------------|
| iterate commit (in worktree) | 164 | evt-ecf57fd9 (stale base) | no |
| merge-of-main commit | 165 | evt-18779597 | no |
| HEAD (after PR merge) | 165 | evt-18779597 | no |
| MAIN-tree working copy | 166 | evt-126ed67f | YES (uncommitted) |

1. `finalize_iterate.py` records the event by **appending to the main tree's**
   `shipwright_events.jsonl`. The events-log path resolves to the git
   *common-dir* (main tree), NOT the worktree -- even when
   `SHIPWRIGHT_PROJECT_ROOT` is exported to the worktree absolute path (the
   env override is honored for decision-drops / changelog-drops / iterate
   entries, but the events append still hits the main tree).
2. The iterate's F6 commit happens **in the worktree**, whose
   `shipwright_events.jsonl` is a different (stale-from-branch-base) file, and
   the F6 explicit `git add` list never includes `shipwright_events.jsonl`.
   So the event cannot enter the PR.
3. Net: the canonical `work_completed` event exists only as an uncommitted
   line in the main tree. `git log -S <evt-id>` may falsely appear to find it
   in the iterate commit -- that match is the event id echoed in the staged
   `.shipwright/agent_docs/iterates/<run_id>.json` + `session_handoff.md`,
   NOT in `shipwright_events.jsonl`.

## Reproduction

1. Start any iterate (worktree is created by `setup_iterate_worktree.py`).
2. Run finalization through F5b (`finalize_iterate.py`).
3. `cd` to the MAIN tree, `git status` -> `M shipwright_events.jsonl`,
   `git diff shipwright_events.jsonl` shows the +work_completed line.
4. The iterate branch / PR contains no events.jsonl change.

## Proposed fixes (pick one; (a) preferred)

- **(a) Honor the worktree for the events append.** Make
  `finalize_iterate.py` write the `work_completed` event to the SAME project
  root used for the other artifacts (respect `SHIPWRIGHT_PROJECT_ROOT` /
  `--project-root` for the events log too, not the git common-dir). Then F6
  stages it like every other artifact and it ships in the PR. Cleanest;
  removes the F7 backfill entirely for worktree iterates.
- **(b) Stage events.jsonl in F6.** Have the iterate F6 commit step always
  `git add shipwright_events.jsonl` when dirty in the worktree. Only works if
  (a) is also done (the event must be in the worktree copy first).
- **(c) Make F7 mandatory.** Drop the "skip unless replaying" carve-out so the
  `chore(events)` commit always runs post-merge. Keeps the main-tree-append
  model but guarantees the commit. Least clean (extra commit per iterate,
  direct-to-main).

## Affected files (shipwright shared tooling)

- `shared/scripts/tools/finalize_iterate.py` (events-append path / root
  resolution) -- primary.
- `shared/scripts/tools/record_event.py` (F7 out-of-band recorder) -- if (c).
- The iterate SKILL F6/F7 reference docs (`shipwright-iterate` plugin:
  `references/F6.md`, `references/F7.md`) -- update the staging/commit rule.
- `check_iterate_isolation.py` -- consider asserting the events.jsonl entry is
  committed (not just present) at F11 to fail-closed on this class of drift.

## Acceptance criteria for the fix

- AC1: After a worktree iterate's F6 commit, `HEAD:shipwright_events.jsonl`
  in the merged result contains the `work_completed` event for that run_id.
- AC2: The main tree's `shipwright_events.jsonl` is clean (`git status` shows
  no `M`) after finalization -- no orphaned uncommitted lines.
- AC3: No separate `chore(events)` backfill commit is required for a normal
  worktree iterate.
- AC4: `verify_iterate_finalization.py` "events.jsonl has commit" check is
  tightened to assert the event is in a COMMIT, not just the working copy.

## Evidence

- Consuming project: shipwright-webui, run_id
  `iterate-2026-05-27-transcript-renderer-scroll`, event `evt-126ed67f`,
  commit `7573e84a00cd8984ecc53455bca42c32e6725be3` (PR #81, merged
  2026-05-29). The event is uncommitted in shipwright-webui's main tree at
  time of writing.
- Related, same mechanism: ADR decision-drops also resolve to the main tree
  (mitigated there because `decision-drops/` is gitignored + consumed on main
  at /shipwright-changelog time). The events.jsonl case is worse because the
  file is TRACKED, so the drift is a real uncommitted-diff, not a benign
  local-only artifact.

title: F-group / source=compliance triage items never auto-dismiss outside /shipwright-compliance
detail: GAP: audit_detector.run(emit_to_triage=True) is the ONLY path that runs mirror_findings_to_triage (the auto-dismiss-when-finding-cleared logic in plugins/shipwright-compliance/scripts/audit/audit_detector.py). That run() is invoked ONLY by the explicit /shipwright-compliance skill. No Stop hook, SessionStart hook, finalize_iterate, or update_compliance.py calls it. Consequence: any source=compliance triage item (F2/F4/F5/F6/F7, B-group, etc.) stays in status=triage until someone manually runs /shipwright-compliance, even after the underlying finding is fixed. EVIDENCE: in shipwright-webui, F4-F7 lingered ~24h after PR #55 cleared them (had to dismiss manually); an F2 item sat stale from 2026-05-14 for over a week. Every OTHER triage producer (phaseQuality via audit_phase_quality_on_stop, github via import_github_findings SessionStart, test-failure + sbom via update_compliance) has a frequent automatic trigger; the compliance-audit producer is the lone exception. NOT solved by PR #78/#79 — those fixed E1-E5 compliance-MD STALENESS false-positives via single-producer + snapshot-provenance, a different concern; they do not touch the F-group emit/dismiss path. RECOMMENDED FIX (Option A1 from the 2026-05-23 design discussion): add shared/scripts/hooks/audit_compliance_on_stop.py modeled on audit_phase_quality_on_stop.py (same Stop contract: never-block, idempotent per HEAD-sha+session, greenfield-safe, opt-out via SHIPWRIGHT_COMPLIANCE_AUDIT_ON_STOP=0). It calls audit_detector.run(emit_to_triage=True). Wire into plugins/shipwright-iterate + plugins/shipwright-changelog hooks.json Stop chains, ordered AFTER finalize + phase_quality and BEFORE aggregate_triage_on_stop. MUST run the FULL audit (groups A-G): mirror_findings_to_triage auto-dismiss is groupless, so running only group F would erroneously dismiss B7/B2/etc. (scoped auto-dismiss = a separate follow-up, Option A2). Do NOT touch the #78 snapshot-provenance E-staleness machinery. Worktree note: triage.jsonl is gitignored + per-worktree, so the hook only cleans the local tree it fires in; cross-worktree triage sync is out of scope (Option C, separate iterate). Priority is now MEDIUM not HIGH because #78 removed the loud recurring E-staleness noise; F-group items are rarer (only on real ADR/CLAUDE.md/architecture hygiene regressions).

reason: Fixed: events.jsonl is now a per-tree, PR-committed artifact (PR #111, iterate-2026-05-29-events-jsonl-worktree-commit). AC1-AC4 all met and verified on origin/main; evt committed in HEAD, main tree clean, no chore(events) backfill, verifier asserts committed.

title: Framework .gitignore artifact rules do not propagate to consuming projects (no canonical template; adopt/project only check coverage)
detail:
## Gap

Framework-added .gitignore rules for .shipwright/ artifacts do NOT propagate to consuming projects — neither existing nor newly-adopted ones.

Root cause:
- shared/templates/shipwright-gitignore.template is a 3-line orphan (loop.lock + runs/ only) and is applied by nobody.
- /shipwright-adopt only CHECKS coverage (gitignore_check.py majority_gitignored ratio + check_paths_against_gitignore report) and hard-enforces only .env.local (secrets). It does NOT write the canonical .shipwright artifact-ignore block (the !/.shipwright/agent_docs/ whitelist + decision-drops/visual/runtime/ re-excludes).
- /shipwright-project writes no .gitignore entries at all.
- Shipwright own .gitignore is hand-maintained; runtime/ was added by ADR-089 (2026-05-27) and is drift-guarded by test_runtime_dir_gitignored.py — but that test covers the FRAMEWORK repo only (REPO_ROOT=shipwright), not consuming projects or any template.

Symptom (found 2026-05-29): shipwright-webui missed /.shipwright/agent_docs/runtime/ → the 3 Stop-hook runtime files (build_dashboard/session_handoff/triage_inbox) showed as untracked clutter. Patched manually in webui (commit f6e34a6). That patch is a symptom-fix; this item is the systemic fix.

## Proposed fix
1. Flesh out shared/templates/shipwright-gitignore.template into the FULL canonical .shipwright artifact-ignore block (SSoT) incl. runtime/.
2. Make /shipwright-adopt AND /shipwright-project idempotently MERGE that block into the target .gitignore (add missing lines, no duplicates) — not just check coverage.
3. Drift-test: template <-> framework .gitignore artifact block stay congruent (a future ADR adding a gitignored dir must update the template -> auto-propagates).
4. Optional: adopt/iterate gitignore self-heal that back-fills missing canonical rules into EXISTING projects (would have caught webui runtime/).

## Affected
shared/templates/shipwright-gitignore.template, plugins/shipwright-adopt/scripts/lib/gitignore_check.py (+ a new writer), plugins/shipwright-project scaffolding, new drift-test under shared/tests/.

title: WebUI triage flip does not regenerate triage_inbox.md (Stop-hook-only trigger)
detail:
Gap: A WebUI triage status flip (dismiss/promote/snooze) appends a status event to <project>/.shipwright/triage.jsonl (so the live view via GET /api/triage/:id is correct), but does NOT regenerate the committed .shipwright/agent_docs/triage_inbox.md. That markdown is refreshed ONLY by the Stop hook aggregate_triage_on_stop.py (registered LAST in plugins/shipwright-iterate/hooks/hooks.json), which fires at Claude-session end -- never on a WebUI/browser action (no Claude session is involved). So between a WebUI triage action and the next Claude-session Stop in that project, the WebUI live view and triage_inbox.md diverge.

Evidence (shipwright-webui, observed 2026-05-30): triage_inbox.md header 'Auto-generated 2026-05-27T07:43'; latest triage.jsonl status event 2026-05-27T15:01; no regeneration since, despite later WebUI activity.

Why it matters: triage_inbox.md is git-tracked and is the anchor target for compliance RTM deep-links (FAIL -> [trg-XXX](../agent_docs/triage_inbox.md#trg-XXX), rtm_generator.py). A stale snapshot means RTM links point to cards already dismissed, or 404 the anchor for items appended after the last Stop.

Scope: framework (shipwright monorepo), NOT the webui repo -- the regeneration trigger + render logic live in shared/scripts/tools/aggregate_triage.py + shared/scripts/hooks/aggregate_triage_on_stop.py. The WebUI (read-mostly observer) has no path to invoke it.

Candidate fixes (decide during the run):
A) Instant sync -- expose aggregate_triage as a thin invokable the WebUI server calls (subprocess) after each status flip. Keeps Python as the single render source of truth. Cost: python-runtime dependency in the Hono server + Windows subprocess fragility + expanded webui write surface (triage_inbox.md becomes a webui-written file). REJECT a TS reimplementation of render_markdown (guaranteed drift vs the canonical Python renderer).
B) Framework-only -- leave regeneration at Stop time but make the staleness explicit/bounded: WebUI surfaces 'snapshot may lag; live view is authoritative', and/or add a standalone regenerate entrypoint (CLI / cron). Lower effort, no instant sync.

Recommendation: B is proportional -- the WebUI live view is already the authoritative inbox; triage_inbox.md is a derived cache. Choose A only if RTM deep-link freshness BETWEEN sessions is a hard requirement.

Do NOT 'remove the item from triage.jsonl' -- the log is append-only / event-sourced by design (audit trail of who dismissed what and why). The current dismiss behavior (append a status event, last-status-wins on read) is correct and must stay.

Filed from a shipwright-webui session on 2026-05-30 at user request (Sven).
launchPayload:
Framework change in the shipwright monorepo. Entry points:
- shared/scripts/tools/aggregate_triage.py (render_markdown)
- shared/scripts/hooks/aggregate_triage_on_stop.py (Stop-time trigger)
- plugins/shipwright-iterate/hooks/hooks.json (Stop chain)
Follow shared/prompts/writing-plugin.md. After editing plugins/shared, run:
  bash scripts/update-marketplace.sh && uv run scripts/check_plugin_cache_sync.py --strict

reason: By design: triage_inbox.md is a derived cache. The RTM (rtm_generator.py:_open_triage_by_fr) and the live WebUI view both read triage.jsonl directly (last-status-wins), so the cache lag is cosmetic and no RTM link breaks. Rejected option A (instant sync); option B regenerate CLI (aggregate_triage.py --project-root) already exists. Full rationale in Claude memory: triage-inbox-md-derived-cache (project_triage_inbox_md_derived_cache.md).

title: CI quick-wins: un-swallow integration-test failures + fix F821 Path import
detail: TWO verified-safe ci.yml/test fixes, bundle into ONE iterate (no debt-paydown needed). (1) .github/workflows/ci.yml line ~74: the integration-tests step ends in '|| true', so integration FAILURES are swallowed and CI stays green. Verified: integration-tests currently 136 passed / 0 failed, so removing '|| true' is risk-free and makes the layer actually gate. (2) shared/tests/test_events_log.py: 14x ruff F821 'Undefined name Path' — the file uses Path only in lazy annotations (has 'from __future__ import annotations') without importing it; add 'from pathlib import Path'. Not a runtime bug (file passes) but removes the F821 noise so lint enforcement (separate item) is cleaner. AC: '|| true' gone from integration step; ruff F821 count = 0; integration + test_events_log green.

title: Wire shared/ tests into CI (collection-collision + 4 stale shared tests)
detail: ROOT systemic gap: ci.yml plugin-test loop only iterates plugins/*/tests/ + integration-tests/, so shared/scripts/**/tests/ and shared/tests/ NEVER run in CI (this is why the 7 record_event tests fixed in iterate-2026-05-30-record-event-test-failures rotted unseen). Wiring is non-trivial in TWO ways. (a) Collection collision: 'pytest shared/tests shared/scripts/tests shared/scripts/tools/tests' together raises 11 ModuleNotFoundError (duplicate top-level 'tests' package between shared/tests/ and shared/scripts/tests/). Workaround: run each dir as a SEPARATE pytest invocation (like the per-plugin loop), or rename/namespace the packages. (b) Running per-dir reveals 4 MORE stale/red shared tests that must be fixed first, else the new CI step is born red: shared/scripts/tests/test_validate_env.py::TestValidateBuild::test_all_vars_present + ::test_partial_vars; shared/tests/test_architecture_md_reflects_arch_impact.py::test_every_arch_impact_drop_has_architecture_md_entry; shared/tests/test_artifact_path_canon.py::test_no_legacy_artifact_paths[compliance-migrated]. AC: those 4 root-caused+fixed (stale vs real?), shared dirs collect+pass per-dir, ci.yml runs them. Supersedes/enriches trg-f363b1ab.

title: Enforce ruff lint in CI (pay down 262 findings, then un-neuter)
detail: ci.yml line ~33: 'uv run ruff check . || true' AND 'continue-on-error: true' — lint is DOUBLE-neutralized, gates nothing, despite 'lint' being in the job name. Current state: ruff check . reports 262 findings (sample: 14x F821 undefined-name [all in test_events_log.py, see quick-wins item], 12x E741 ambiguous-var-name, E701/E401/F811/E731). 176 are --fix auto-fixable. PLAN: (1) decide rule set (ruff config in pyproject? currently none beyond defaults), (2) auto-fix the safe ones, manually resolve the rest (esp. confirm the F821 are all annotation-only / not real), (3) remove BOTH '|| true' and 'continue-on-error: true' so lint actually gates. Large debt-paydown — own iterate. AC: ruff check . exits 0; ci.yml lint step gates (no || true / continue-on-error).

title: Introduce Python type-checking in CI (the job name claims type but none exists)
detail: The ci.yml job is named 'Python (lint + type + test)' but there is NO type-checker configured ANYWHERE (no mypy/pyright/ty in pyproject.toml, no CI step). The 'type' claim is fiction. PLAN: pick a checker (recommend Astral 'ty' for uv-native speed, or pyright), add minimal config (start lenient: check shared/ + plugins/*/scripts/, ignore tests), pay down the surfaced errors incrementally (likely many on a large untyped codebase), then add a gating CI step. Large — own iterate, can be staged (e.g. type-check only changed files first). AC: a type-checker runs in CI and gates; the job name is truthful.

title: CI extension guard: assert all test dirs covered + no silent un-gating
detail: WHY THIS EXISTS: ci.yml was authored as 'dormant' (commit 4107a6b, early-access) with || true + continue-on-error baked in, then only PARTIALLY hardened (2671c69/d85210f added scanners+CI-aware-tests+CodeQL guard but left lint/integration/shared/type loose). No guard prevents this recurring. BUILD a meta-check (shared test or a tiny CI-lint script run in CI) that fails when: (a) any test directory under plugins/*/tests, shared/**/tests, integration-tests/ is NOT referenced by a CI pytest invocation (catches a new plugin/shared dir silently uncovered — the 'CI extension' guard you asked for); (b) any gating step (test/lint/type) carries '|| true' or 'continue-on-error: true' outside a documented allowlist (allow: codeql analyze + security SARIF-upload/scan steps, which are intentionally non-gating on private repos). ALSO fold in two known loose gates to verify/track: security.yml critical-gate reads 0 criticals when findings.json is ABSENT (scanner crash => silent green) — make missing-file fail or warn-loud; codeql.yml analyze has continue-on-error (documented, private-repo) that MUST be removed at public launch. AC: the guard fails on an injected || true and on an uncovered test dir; security missing-findings no longer passes silently; codeql launch-gate tracked.

reason: Won't-do (pragmatic, solo/small-team target): full static type-checking on a mature un-annotated scripts codebase is a perpetual annotation tax for marginal ROI. Tests + ruff F-rules (undefined-name, redefinition, unused) already cover the realistic bug surface. The only real issue — the misleading 'type' in the CI job name — is folded into the lint item (rename job to lint+test).
Re-scoped: enforcing the full ruff default set (262 findings, mostly style noise) is over-rigor for the target audience. Superseded by a pragmatic curated-ruleset item.

title: Enforce a pragmatic ruff ruleset in CI (bug-focused, not full default set)
detail: ci.yml line ~33 neuters lint twice ('ruff check . || true' + 'continue-on-error: true') so it gates nothing, despite 'lint' in the job name. PRAGMATIC plan for solo/small-team (NOT the full 262-finding default set): (1) add a ruff config in pyproject.toml selecting a BUG-FOCUSED ruleset — pyflakes F (F821 undefined-name, F811 redefinition, F401 unused-import, F-string issues) plus a light, high-signal slice of E/W; explicitly skip purely-cosmetic rules that just create churn. (2) 'ruff check --fix' the freebies (~176 auto-fixable), manually clear the rest of the SELECTED set. (3) remove BOTH '|| true' and 'continue-on-error: true' so lint gates. (4) While in this job: rename it from 'Python (lint + type + test)' to 'Python (lint + test)' since type-checking is a deliberate won't-do (see dismissed trg-84f204ba). AC: ruff check . exits 0 against the curated ruleset; lint step gates; job name no longer claims type.

title: plugin_sync_reminder_on_stop files its triage item in the worktree log (ephemeral), not the main repo -- does not survive iterate-worktree sessions
detail:
## Bug

`shared/scripts/hooks/plugin_sync_reminder_on_stop.py` (SP4 Stop hook) appends its `source=plugin-sync` / dedupKey `plugin-sync:cache-drift` triage item via `_emit_triage(project_root, ...)`, where `project_root = _resolve_project_root()` -> `lib.project_root.resolve_project_root()`. In an iterate-worktree session that resolves to the WORKTREE root (`.worktrees/<slug>`), so the item lands in `.worktrees/<slug>/.shipwright/triage.jsonl` -- a gitignored, per-tree, ephemeral log discarded on `git worktree remove` after PR merge. It never reaches the durable main-repo `.shipwright/triage.jsonl` that the WebUI / RTM read.

Result: the docstring promise ('a triage item has been filed so it survives the session') is FALSE for iterate-worktree sessions -- the durable follow-up handle is lost on worktree cleanup. The block-once Stop reminder banner still fires (the live nag works); only the audit/follow-up item evaporates.

## Evidence (2026-05-30, observed live)
- iterate-2026-05-30-gitignore-canon-propagation ran in `.worktrees/gitignore-canon-propagation`.
- Its reminder item landed there as trg-b860a99e; `grep -c plugin-sync` on the main-tree `.shipwright/triage.jsonl` = 0.
- Contrast: the gap item trg-afce5459 (source=manual) correctly lived in the main-tree log and survived for the user to ask about.

## Root cause
`_resolve_project_root()` returns the active SDLC project context (the worktree) -- correct for the reminder banner + once-per-session sentinel, but WRONG for the durable triage append. Triage items are an append-only audit trail and must live in the MAIN repo log.

## Proposed fix
Resolve the MAIN repo root for the `_emit_triage` append only -- mirror the decision-drop pattern (`shared/scripts/lib/events_log.resolve_main_repo_root`, git-common-dir) that already writes decision-drops to the main tree from inside a worktree. Keep `_resolve_project_root()` (worktree) for the banner + `_claim_reminded` sentinel. e.g. `_emit_triage(resolve_main_repo_root(project_root) or project_root, rel_paths)`.

## Affected
- shared/scripts/hooks/plugin_sync_reminder_on_stop.py (_emit_triage target)
- reuse shared/scripts/lib/events_log.resolve_main_repo_root (existing main-root resolver)
- new test under shared/tests/ (worktree fixture: assert the item is appended to the main-repo log, not the worktree log)

## Notes
- Monorepo-dev only (is_monorepo gate) -> end-user projects unaffected.
- The mis-filed item from this session (trg-b860a99e) was dismissed in the worktree log; it vanishes with the worktree.
- Filed at user request (Sven), 2026-05-30, from the iterate-2026-05-30-gitignore-canon-propagation session.

Three-stage cascade spec-reviewer (HARD-GATE) -> code-reviewer -> conditional doubt-reviewer; all acceptance criteria empirically verified (32 structural tests + 4 live behavioral probes). title: Track .shipwright/triage.jsonl (mirror shipwright_events.jsonl) — stop machine-local backlog + fix WebUI≠snapshot divergence
detail: Campaign A-E. The tracked triage_inbox.md diverges completely from the WebUI because triage.jsonl is gitignored everywhere and the per-worktree snapshot is rendered from an ephemeral SBOM-only jsonl. Decision: git-track triage.jsonl mirroring events.jsonl (jsonl=SSoT, .md=derived view). Prereqs: A=stabilize SBOM cluster identity (B-churn), B=GC dismissed pile. Then C=tracking flip (template negation + scaffolder self-heal + churn_merge allowlist + resolver + F6 git add + finalize simplify), D=adopt/project wiring, E=docs+monorepo migration. Full analysis + ACs + gotchas in the evidence-path plan doc. Start: /shipwright-iterate from proposed-track-triage-jsonl.md, sub-iterate A first.

title: Compliance detective audit drifted from event/release model — realign B7+GroupE+A5-invocation+FR-gate [campaign 2026-06-02-compliance-detective-realign]
detail:
STATUS 2026-06-02: C1 + C2 + B MERGED to main (PR #142, merge a8793c69) and cache-synced (13 plugins in sync). B = B7 recognizes chore(release) (Rule D, narrow). REMAINING: C3 (FR-gate finalize bypass + D3 same-event), C4 (sub-iterate A = backfill events for the 4 ci/docs drift commits + G2 board + reopen FR link, webui repo). The monorepo Group E + webui B7/A5.0 symptoms should auto-dismiss on next producer run. C1 (B7 Run-ID↔adr_id linkage + Group E release/changelog snapshot recognition) and C2 (A5 SKIP-not-FAIL on missing pyyaml + `uv run --with pyyaml` on the iterate/changelog Stop hooks) done; 591 compliance + 28 shared hook tests pass, ruff clean. Real-data validation: monorepo Group E now GREEN (snapshot=a0aa1e62, any_stale=False); WebUI B7 no longer flags the real feature 26ea506 (8→5 uncovered, residual = ci/docs/chore(release) only). C3 + C4 remain.

ROOT CAUSE: the detective audit + its background invocation drifted from two framework redesigns — (1) iterate-2026-05-29-events-jsonl-worktree-commit: work_completed events ship commit:"" BY DESIGN and link via the F6 commit's Run-ID: footer <-> the event's adr_id; (2) /shipwright-changelog regenerates tracked agent-doc/compliance MDs as chore(release): with NO Run-ID: trailer. Two detective checks assumed the old model and the audit's uv-run invocation assumed a Python project env, producing recurring FALSE-POSITIVE compliance findings in BOTH repos that the #132 per-project disabled_checks band-aid only MASKED.

VERIFIED EVIDENCE (this session):
- B7 (group_b._check_b7) matched only the event commit field; 26ea506 carries "Run-ID: iterate-2026-06-02-campaigns-board-lane" and evt-177f8389 has the matching adr_id. FIXED in C1: B7 now also matches Run-ID footer <-> adr_id (commit-field fallback retained).
- Group E (audit_staleness.find_snapshot_commit) matched only git log --grep=Run-ID:; a0aa1e62 chore(release) regenerated the MDs without that trailer. FIXED in C1: find_snapshot_commit now recognizes chore(release) snapshots too (manual chore(compliance) regens still flagged).
- A5.0: audit_compliance_on_stop runs `uv run` from the project root; non-Python adopt repos lack pyyaml -> import yaml failed. FIXED in C2: group_a5 degrades to SKIP (not FAIL) when yaml is absent + the iterate/changelog Stop hooks invoke `uv run --with pyyaml`.
- D3 (group_d._check_d3) requires a strictly-later affected_frs event; FR-01.33 is introduced+delivered in the SAME event evt-177f8389 -> perpetually pending. (C3.)
- D5: reopen event evt-83b9b73f (feature, spec_impact=ADD, no FR) should have been blocked by the ADR-059 FR-gate, but finalize_iterate._record_event bypasses it. C3 TRACE RESOLVED: the bypass is finalize_iterate._record_event -> append_event (no FR-gate); the real SHA is just the F7 --commit path, not a separate writer. AC-1 insert point pinned at finalize_iterate.py:261. (C3.)

SCOPE -> campaign .shipwright/planning/iterate/campaigns/2026-06-02-compliance-detective-realign/. Sub-iterates (ordered): C1 audit-run-id-provenance (DONE, uncommitted); C2 audit-invocation-resilience (DONE, uncommitted); C3 fr-linkage-lifecycle (FR-gate finalize bypass + D3 same-event delivery); C4 webui-data-config (G2 board + reopen FR link — webui repo, LAST, after update-marketplace.sh sync).

DECISION (2026-06-02, user): do NOT exclude commit types from B7 — B7 exists to surface commits that bypassed iterate; type-suppression would hide exactly that drift. The residual 5 (ci/docs/chore(release)) are CORRECT, transient findings (age out at the next release tag); reconcile as DATA in C4, not via the check.

CLOSES (auto-dismiss when work lands + synced): trg-8747213b (monorepo Group E) -> C1; trg-2bce4cc6 (webui A5.0/B7/D3/D5/G2) -> C1/C2/C3/C4. Those two are producer-owned compliance-backlog SYMPTOMS; THIS item is the stable engineering anchor the campaign expands.

Plan-first recommended for C3 (write-side gate, highest blast radius — the finalize gate can block iterate completion). Full spec: campaign.md + sub-iterates/.

title: Campaign lifecycle status (draft -> active -> complete) — make a campaign identifiable as running vs not
detail: Add a campaign-level status field so consumers (WebUI Campaigns lane + CLI campaign_progress summary) can tell whether a campaign is started or just planned. Today only per-step status exists; done/total is derived, so a freshly-created/hand-authored campaign (all steps pending) is indistinguishable from one that is actively running, and it shows up everywhere immediately. Changes: (1) campaign_init.py init_campaign writes top-level status:'draft' into status.json AND status: draft into the campaign.md frontmatter. (2) campaign_progress.py: add a 'start' (or set-status) subcommand that sets the top-level status to 'active'; when update-status makes all sub_iterates complete, set status:'complete'; the 'summary' command prints the top-level status. (3) The autonomous campaign loop sets status='active' at run start and 'complete' at finalize. (4) Backward-compat: a missing status field means legacy — consumers fall back to the current done<total behavior so nothing existing breaks. Lifecycle: draft (planned, lives only in triage) -> active (started, shown on the board) -> complete (hidden). Counterpart already being built in shipwright-webui: the Campaigns lane filters the board on status==='active' (draft/complete hidden), with the legacy no-status fallback. Decision recorded in webui design session 2026-06-03 (Option B, draft hidden = triage-only).

title: Harden compliance A5: verify the security critical-gate actually BLOCKS, not just that the step exists
detail: Group A5 audits the deployed .github/workflows/security.yml by grepping for the shipwright-critical-gate step id (CRITICAL_GATE_STEP_ID) — it confirms the step is PRESENT but never that it WORKS. The 2026-06-04 false-green was a gate whose step existed yet could never block (read SARIF security-severity at result-level instead of rule-level). Harden A5 to execute the gate shell against fixture SARIF (rule-level >=9.0 -> blocks; empty/invalid -> fail-closed; clean -> passes) so a structurally-broken DEPLOYED gate is caught. Root-cause fix shipped PR #144; shared/tests/test_security_critical_gate.py already covers the TEMPLATE, A5 should cover the deployed file.

title: security.yml fetch-depth:0 comment is vestigial — no scanner is diff/history-aware
detail: shared/templates/github-actions/security.yml.template (and the deployed shipwright-webui copy) checkout with 'fetch-depth: 0  # full history for diff-aware secret scans', but NO scanner uses git history/diff: gitleaks runs --no-git (filesystem), semgrep 'scan --config auto .' and trivy 'fs .' scan the working tree. The full-history fetch is unnecessary AND correlated with a transient merge-ref gitleaks false positive on shipwright-webui PR #103 (cafebabe:deadbeef surfaced from a stale merge ref, now allowlisted via .gitleaks.toml). Either drop to fetch-depth:1 (current tree only) or fix the misleading comment. Low priority.

title: Gitleaks smoke test silently SKIPS in CI: same-version gitleaks finds the synthetic PEM locally but 0 in CI
detail: plugins/shipwright-security/tests/test_oss_backend_smoke.py::test_gitleaks_detect_scans_committed_shipwright_dir uses a positive-control gate: if gitleaks does not find src/main_key.pem it pytest.skip()s (allow-silent-skip). It SKIPS in every CI run (e.g. 26941507522, 'Got paths: []') while semgrep+trivy smoke tests pass -- so gitleaks' real detection + node_modules-exclusion contract is NEVER exercised in CI. Investigated 2026-06-04: reproduced the synthetic fixture (BEGIN/END RSA PRIVATE KEY + short body) committed in a git repo and ran the EXACT _run_gitleaks invocation (gitleaks detect -s <repo> --config <generated allowlist with [extend] useDefault=true>). LOCAL gitleaks 8.21.2 (Windows) detects it (1 finding, rule=private-key, entropy 4.64) WITH and WITHOUT the allowlist config; CI gitleaks 8.21.2 (Linux) finds 0. Ruled out: fixture content, allowlist config. NOT reproducible on this Windows host -> needs a Linux repro / a temporary CI diagnostic step. Candidate causes: win-vs-linux binary default-ruleset divergence; gitleaks git-mode behavior on a root commit in the CI env; or _run_tool error handling swallowing a gitleaks failure. Possible direction: if the positive control cannot be made reliable, the silent skip should become a CI-fail per the ADR-044 silent-skip discipline so the coverage hole is visible rather than hidden. Same win/linux gitleaks divergence was also seen with the sidekiq-secret rule on shipwright-webui PR #103 (cafebabe:deadbeef).

title: Durable producer-maintained campaign status (tracked SSoT)
detail: Campaign per-sub status is read by the WebUI from an untracked, never-committed status.json; the tracked campaign.md table is write-once 'pending' (campaign_progress updates status.json only) -> board shows stale/wrong status on a fresh clone / deployed WebUI. Proposal (Option A recommended): producer maintains the tracked campaign.md table as the SSoT; status.json demoted to a local cache. Plan doc: .shipwright/planning/iterate/proposed-tracked-campaign-status.md. title: Durable producer-maintained campaign status (tracked SSoT)
detail: WebUI reads per-sub campaign status from status.json, which is authoritative per the campaign-store.ts contract + PR #143 (producer-owned) — but it is untracked/never-committed, and the tracked campaign.md table is write-once 'pending' -> board is wrong on a fresh clone / deployed WebUI. RECOMMENDED: track status.json (per-tree commit + by-id SEMANTIC churn resolver, since it is a mutable object not an append-only log), mirroring the triage.jsonl/events.jsonl precedent -> ZERO WebUI change (status.json already authoritative). Plan-phase spike: derive status from the already-tracked events.jsonl instead (cleanest if the sub-iterate<->run_id<->event linkage is clean). REJECTED: promoting the campaign.md markdown table to status SSoT (contradicts the status.json-authoritative contract; would invert a 2-day-old WebUI contract; markdown-as-SSoT is the retired anti-pattern, cf. triage_inbox.md). Plan doc: .shipwright/planning/iterate/proposed-tracked-campaign-status.md. Surfaced by detective-realign C1/C2.

title: Bloat session-marker not worktree-aware: false Stop-block after ADR-documented baseline bump in a worktree iterate
detail: bloat_gate_on_stop.py + the PostToolUse bloat recorder store MAIN-root-relative paths (.worktrees/<slug>/...) in the session marker .shipwright/locks/bloat_pending.<sid>.json, then look them up in shipwright_bloat_baseline.json (which keys repo-relative paths, no .worktrees/ prefix). For a worktree iterate that grows an ALREADY-baselined file, this (a) mis-classifies the anti-ratchet as delta=crossing at write and (b) false-blocks Stop as a not-in-baseline crossing at read -- even when the committed baseline + the CI anti-ratchet gate (which read the worktree baseline) correctly PASS. Repro: iterate-2026-06-05-fr-linkage-lifecycle (campaign 2026-06-02 C3) bumped test_finalize_iterate.py 306->464 + test_audit_groups_a_d.py 748->789 under ADR-096; CI 'Anti-ratchet + allowlist diff' passed but the Stop gate blocked on '.worktrees/.../test_finalize_iterate.py'. Workaround used: rm the stale 1h-TTL session marker. Fix: strip the '.worktrees/<slug>/' prefix (or resolve the worktree baseline + worktree-relative path) before the baseline lookup in BOTH the recorder and bloat_gate_on_stop (_re_measure_oversize/_baseline_map).

title: OSS scanner: distinguish fatal scanner error from clean 0-findings
detail: Follow-up from iterate-2026-06-05-gitleaks-report-path. _run_tool now surfaces stderr when an expect_nonzero tool (gitleaks) yields an empty/unparseable report, but _run_gitleaks -> OSSBackend.scan still treats 'scanner fataled' identically to 'clean: 0 findings'. A gitleaks fatal (exit 1: dubious-ownership/corrupt-repo) or truncated report degrades to a green secrets leg -- same failure CLASS as the report-path bug just fixed. Propagate a scanner-error marker from _run_tool through scan() so the threshold/report layer flags a degraded scan. Applies to semgrep/trivy empty-stdout too.

title: Triage-store amend event: faithful SBOM cluster body re-render under membership drift
detail: Sub-iterate A (iterate-2026-06-05-sbom-cluster-stable-identity) made the SBOM cluster dedup-key signature-only, so the cluster id is stable under membership drift. But the triage store is append-only (append+status events; body fields written once at append), so an open cluster's body (workspace list + launch payload) reflects membership-at-first-emit until the signature fully resolves and re-emerges. Faithful re-render needs a new 'amend' event in shared/scripts/triage.py (overlay title/detail/launchPayload in read_all_items) PLUS the WebUI Triage-Inbox consumer. Separable shared-contract + cross-repo change. Motivating symptom: a newly-undeclared workspace joining an open cluster is missing from that cluster's launch-payload for-loop until re-emit. See campaign 2026-06-05-track-triage-jsonl sub-iterate A scope note.

title: shipwright-adopt: scaffold .gitleaks.toml allowlist + harden security.yml.template
detail: Adopt copies security.yml.template (gitleaks runs --no-git with no --config, relying on an auto-loaded .gitleaks.toml at repo root) but NEVER scaffolds that .gitleaks.toml. Result: every newly-adopted repo's first Security Scan goes RED on the universal transient gitleaks 'sidekiq-secret' FP (cafebabe:deadbeef) — proven on leadwright 2026-06-07 (runs 27086046885 red -> 27086178138 green after adding .gitleaks.toml). Also: security.yml.template lags the monorepo's own security.yml + webui on hardening (gitleaks via wget|tar without SHA256 verify; peter-evans@v4 tag not commit-SHA). Fix plan: add shared/templates/github-actions/gitleaks.toml.template (= webui allowlist), wire generate_adoption_artifacts.py to copy it to <root>/.gitleaks.toml + test (mirror test_security_workflow_scaffold.py), fold gitleaks SHA-verify + peter-evans SHA-pin into security.yml.template, update step-e-artifact-generation.md + run update-marketplace.sh. See .shipwright/planning/iterate/proposed-adopt-gitleaks-allowlist.md.

Cost > benefit. Re-open only if new workspaces regularly join open clusters.

title: triage.jsonl main-tree drift blocks git pull/FF — reconcile-and-commit at integrate/sync
detail: C2 (#156) of the track-triage campaign chose a leak-guard EXEMPTION over the per-tree producer reroute (old C3). The exemption silences the leak-guard but is NOT a commit-path: per-session background producers (plugin-sync Stop-hook, compliance audit, triage_add) append to the tracked, main-repo-root-durable triage.jsonl, leaving UNCOMMITTED main-tree drift with no commit path (worktrees snapshot origin/main, never the dirty main tree -> orphans). The churn resolver only covers committed-vs-committed merges, so 'git merge --ff-only / git pull' against the dirty main tree is BLOCKED (hit 2026-06-07 during post-merge plugin-cache-sync; had to reconcile by hand). events.jsonl avoids this (per-tree pipeline producers). FIX (single iterate, not a campaign): a guarded reconcile_main_triage(project_root) that validates+union-dedups+commits the drift as ONE chore(triage) (B7-exempt) BEFORE any FF/pull, wired into integrate_main.py + the sync path (+ optionally setup_iterate_worktree to close the orphan). Commit lives in TOOLING, not hooks (avoids history-noise/hook-commit/race). Full design + ACs + rejected-alternatives: .shipwright/planning/iterate/proposed-triage-main-tree-drift-reconcile.md

title: Scaffold churn-merge anti-theater machinery (.gitattributes union + resolver) into target/adopted repos
detail: Verified 2026-06-07: the union merge-driver + churn resolver that keep the MONOREPO free of manual merge theater on append-log artifacts (shipwright_events.jsonl, .shipwright/triage.jsonl) are monorepo-local and NOT scaffolded into managed projects. WebUI .gitattributes has no merge=union (git check-attr -> unspecified); adopt scaffolds no .gitattributes; resolve_churn_conflicts.py has 0 copies under plugins/ (only shared/scripts/, imported by integrate_main.py via a monorepo-relative path). => every adopted repo (webui/leadwright/aiportal/end-user) falls back to git default conflict behavior on concurrent triage/events appends. WebUI is the proven first victim (#96-#100 back-merges resolved these files by hand; triage.jsonl tracked since #111 with NO union driver = latent now). FIX (single iterate, medium, NOT a campaign): shared .gitattributes union template fragment; adopt scaffolds it idempotently (gitleaks.toml pattern); iterate self-heal backfills already-adopted repos in one chore commit; AC-5 records resolver-reachability decision (bundle vs union-only). Full design + ACs + rejected-alternatives: .shipwright/planning/iterate/proposed-scaffold-churn-merge-machinery.md. Sibling proposal-triage-main-tree-drift-reconcile.md (active session) covers the separate main-tree drift / git-pull block.

title: Scaffold append-log union merge driver into managed repos

Addressed by iterate-2026-06-07-scaffold-churn-merge-machinery (adopt E.13c + iterate self-heal).

title: triage.jsonl background appends never reach origin (orphan reconcile-to-main)
detail: reconcile_main_triage() folds main-tree background drift into chore(triage) commits on LOCAL main; PR-protected repo never pushes main + iterate worktrees branch from origin/<default>, so the commits are terminal -> local main 6 ahead of origin and growing, appends never reach origin/CI/fresh-clone. PR #169 solved only J1 (main stays pullable), not J2 (delivery). Codex review (2026-06-08) killed the branch-seed+reset fix (lock-race + abandoned-branch data loss) AND the keep-commit+seed fallback (ahead-count still grows unbounded). Correct end-state = re-open the de-scoped C3 per-tree producer reroute: gitignored outbox + sweep-into-PR-branch + dedup-GC + live-view union. Campaign anchor. Plan: .shipwright/planning/iterate/proposed-triage-reconcile-origin-delivery.md
