title: external-review-state hygiene: transient review-gate state inconsistently tracked (ADR-089 leak class)
detail: The external-review gate (mark-review-state.py) writes external_review_state.json + external_code_review_state.json (Branch A/B/C state) into the run's planning dir. RESOLVED DESIGN (investigated 2026-06-09): these markers are TRANSIENT run-scoped state, NOT durable audit evidence. KEY FINDING: rtm.py::collect_external_review_states (lines 234-237) EXPLICITLY SKIPS .shipwright/planning/iterate/ ('iterate runs produce run-scoped markers audited separately via EVENTS, not per-split RTM rows'); the RTM only treats .shipwright/planning/<plan-split>/external_review_state.json as durable evidence. All 6 currently git-tracked copies live under .shipwright/planning/iterate/ (2 top-level + 4 in campaign sub-iterates) => transient, accidentally committed. Handoff + W5 verifiers read only the CURRENT run's marker (live), never historical copies. CORRECT FIX (iterate-scoped, NOT blanket): a blanket **/external_*review_state.json ignore would be a NEAR-MISS — it would also catch the durable plan-split RTM evidence and erase it on fresh clones. Instead add to the gitignore canon (template + framework managed block, congruent): /.shipwright/planning/iterate/**/external_review_state.json and /.shipwright/planning/iterate/**/external_code_review_state.json (the **/ matches the direct top-level copy AND the deeper campaign/sub-iterate copies; it does NOT touch planning/<split>/external_*). Then git rm --cached the 6 tracked copies (gitignore alone does not untrack committed files). Plan-split markers stay tracked = RTM evidence preserved. Propagates to adopted repos via the canon template. Same producer-leak class as ADR-089 / iterate-2026-06-09-idle-main-artifact-hygiene / -audit-report-hygiene.
launchPayload: /shipwright-iterate --type change external-review-state hygiene (trg-60ef91fb): gitignore the TRANSIENT iterate-scoped review markers + untrack the 6 committed copies; KEEP plan-split markers tracked (RTM evidence). Add to canon (template + framework, congruent): /.shipwright/planning/iterate/**/external_review_state.json + /.shipwright/planning/iterate/**/external_code_review_state.json ; git rm --cached the 6 tracked copies under planning/iterate/. DO NOT use a blanket **/ pattern (would erase durable plan-split RTM evidence per rtm.py:234 skip). Tests: gitignore congruence + probe (iterate marker ignored, plan-split marker NOT ignored). expands_triage trg-60ef91fb; dismiss after.

title: artifact-path-canon: exempt generated triage_inbox.md cache from legacy-path lint
detail: The artifact-path-canon Layer-1 lint (test_no_legacy_artifact_paths) scans tracked .md/.py for bare legacy artifact-dir references. .shipwright/agent_docs/triage_inbox.md is a GENERATED cache (regenerated each iterate by aggregate_triage.py from .shipwright/triage.jsonl) that renders arbitrary producer finding detail + launchPayload, which can quote any path verbatim. A finding whose text mentions a bare legacy artifact dir therefore trips the lint on the generated cache, which cannot be fixed in place because it regenerates. FIX: add .shipwright/agent_docs/triage_inbox.md to ALLOWLIST in shared/scripts/lib/artifact_migrations.py for all 4 migrations, same exempt class as .shipwright/compliance/change-history.md and shipwright_test_results.json prose notes. This ratchets the artifact_migrations.py bloat baseline (592 LOC) so a deliberate baseline bump is required -> own small iterate. Exposed by iterate-2026-06-10-triage-dedup-keep-last-append: the unblocked outbox sweep delivered a resolved finding (trg-60ef91fb) whose launchPayload quoted .shipwright/planning/iterate paths into the inbox; worked around there by dismissing the finding. Consider also session_handoff.md + build_dashboard.md (same generated agent-doc trio).

title: Triage live-view: union the gitignored outbox so new items are visible/startable before sweep
detail: UX gap: a freshly-created triage item (manual triage_add or an idle-main background producer) routes to the gitignored per-tree buffer .shipwright/triage.outbox.jsonl and is INVISIBLE in the WebUI until the next iterate-setup sweep folds it into the tracked .shipwright/triage.jsonl. So you cannot see or one-click-start an iterate for the very item that should BE the next iterate (chicken-and-egg). Root cause: the monorepo canonical reader shared/scripts/triage.py::read_all_items ALREADY unions tracked + outbox (two-pass union, D1) and triage_cli.py list shows outbox items; but the WebUI reads only the tracked log (or the derived triage_inbox.md). FIX (two parts): (1) MONOREPO enabler - add a JSON contract triage_cli.py list --json that emits the unioned, reduced item list (single source of truth for union + reduction) so the WebUI consumes it instead of re-parsing JSONL. (2) WEBUI (shipwright-webui repo) - live-union read: render tracked + outbox, badge outbox-only items as Pending-delivery/ships-with-next-iterate, and let the existing Start/Fix CTA act on them (the iterate setup sweep delivers the item into that iterate PR automatically). Do NOT route user/manual items straight to the tracked log to fix visibility - that reintroduces main-tree drift the outbox exists to prevent. Interim: triage_cli.py list already shows the item; an iterate can be started manually and the setup sweep delivers it. NOTE: this is the real D4 (WebUI live-view union); the id trg-55516654 referenced in older notes is a DIFFERENT item (idle-main artifact hygiene).

title: classify_complexity Stage-1 systematically under-classifies: app-domain keywords + hard-coded trivial fall-through
detail: Empirical (45 Stage-1 outputs from sessions 2026-05-10..2026-06-10 vs 50 finalized runs in agent_docs/iterates): Stage 1 returns trivial 64% of the time (scope_keyword_estimate trivial in 33/45), but only 14% of runs finalize trivial (small 44%, medium 42%). The agent's Stage-2 Repo Scout bumps nearly every run upward, so Stage 1 carries near-zero signal in this repo. Root causes: (1) estimate_scope() keyword vocabulary is hard-coded for end-user web apps (spinner/tooltip/dashboard/new page) and never matches monorepo vocabulary (hook, triage, producer, gate, worktree, classifier) -> falls through to 'trivial'; (2) the fall-through default is the lowest rung instead of the project's observed median; (3) no feedback loop although F5c entries record final complexity per run; (4) the few medium/large hits are lexical accidents ('dashboard' in 'compliance dashboard', 'session' in SessionStart -> touches_auth, 'schema' -> touches_migrations). Side effect: a trivial estimate selects the Quick repo scout, so the stage meant to correct the estimate is itself shallowed. PROPOSED FIX (Option 1 + signals transparency): classify() reads the last N=20 finalized complexities from .shipwright/agent_docs/iterates/*.json and uses their median (clamped small..medium) as the fall-through prior when no scope keyword matched; expose signals.prior_source = history|keyword|default and print it in the Run Summary; keep risk-floor logic unchanged; cold start (no entries) keeps today's trivial default. Secondary (separate, optional): per-project scope vocabulary generated at adopt/project time to also fix risk-flag false positives. Estimated scope: small-medium iterate, touches classify_complexity.py + tests + SKILL.md Step E prose.

title: Bloat Stop-gate reads MAIN baseline while re-measuring the WORKTREE file — false-block on a worktree iterate that bumps a baseline (trg-305e2aab gap)
detail: Reproduced 2026-06-10 in PR #184. trg-305e2aab was dismissed as FIXED in #150, but #150 fixed only the PATH-prefix half. bloat_gate_on_stop.main(): current=_re_measure_oversize(root,entry) measures main_root/.worktrees/<slug>/.../file (the WORKTREE file, e.g. 326), but ceiling=_baseline_map(root) loads the MAIN-repo shipwright_bloat_baseline.json (old current, e.g. 318) — my ADR-documented bump to 326 lives in the worktree, committed in the PR, not yet on main. So 326<=318 is false -> anti-ratchet offender -> Stop-block, even though pre-commit + CI anti-ratchet (which read the worktree baseline) PASS. Same baseline-source mismatch in the recorder check_file_size.py at write time (records delta=anti-ratchet against main's baseline). Fix: when the marker path is a .worktrees/<slug>/ path, resolve the ceiling from THAT worktree's baseline (main_root/.worktrees/<slug>/shipwright_bloat_baseline.json), so the re-measured file and its ceiling come from the same tree. Add a regression test reproducing worktree-bump-then-Stop (missing from #150). Workaround today: rm the stale 1h-TTL session marker.

title: Align bloat marker WRITER (check_file_size) to worktree baseline — delta label still main-keyed (harmless after the trg-28e83840 reader fix)
detail: Follow-up to trg-28e83840 (Stop-gate reader fix). The recorder check_file_size.py computes the marker delta (anti-ratchet vs crossing) + was_in_allowlist against the MAIN baseline at write time, even for a .worktrees/<slug>/ path. After the reader fix (bloat_gate_on_stop resolves the ceiling + in_baseline from the worktree's own baseline) this is HARMLESS — the reader re-derives the correct decision regardless of the stored delta label (proven by test_worktree_newly_baselined_file_not_treated_as_crossing). So this is a clarity/consistency cleanup, NOT a safety gap: align the writer to read the worktree baseline (or drop the now-advisory delta/was_in_allowlist from the marker, since the reader recomputes). Found by adversarial review of the trg-28e83840 PR.

title: Campaign status projection writes machine-absolute spec_path (non-portable)
detail: campaign_status projection + campaign_init fill a sub-iterate spec_path as an ABSOLUTE campaign_dir path, so a regenerated/committed status.json carries e.g. C:\01_Development\... — useless on a fresh clone or a Linux WebUI server. Fix: make spec_path repo-relative in BOTH campaign_init and the projection fill (campaign_status_io/campaign_status) + migrate existing tracked campaigns. Deferred N1 from campaign 2026-06-07-tracked-campaign-status (S3/S4); Gemini S2 external review flagged it. Not in any S-spec AC, so filed as a standalone improvement.

Brief: Implement tiered PR-Review via OpenRouter + Custom-Script. Spec: Spec/early-access-readiness-plan.md → B4.5 → Components.

Files to create:
- shared/prompts/pr_reviewer/system/ + user/ (Verzeichnis-Form analog zu code_reviewer/, iterate_reviewer/ — etabliert seit PR #119)
- plugins/shipwright-security/scripts/tools/pr_review.py (~120-150 LOC):
  * fetch PR diff via `gh pr diff`
  * POST to https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions mit model $SHIPWRIGHT_PR_REVIEW_MODEL (default anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6)
  * strict JSON response: {decision: approve|comment|block, summary, blocking[], comments[]}
  * post via `gh pr comment` + optional `gh pr review --request-changes|--comment`
  * exit 0 für approve/comment, 1 für block, 2 für errors
  * Diff > 200k chars: defensiv truncaten + im Body vermerken
- .github/workflows/pr-review.yml: zwei Jobs (decide + review) mit Tier-Filtern
  * decide: external author? sensitive path? labels (skip-pr-review/needs-review)?
  * review: needs: decide, if needs.decide.outputs.needs_review == 'true'
  * Job-Name `PR Review` (Branch-Protection-Required-Check)
  * Fork-PR-Guard: head.repo.full_name == github.repository
- plugins/shipwright-security/tests/test_pr_review_workflow_shape.py + test_pr_review_script.py

Smoke-Tests vor merge (Spec → Phase 2 Steps 5-6):
1. Test-PR aus svroch Account (Tier 2) → review-Job skipped, Required-Check trotzdem grün

title: B4.5-W WebUI — Align PR Review Architecture (OpenRouter + tiered, drop develop)
detail: Aktuell hat shipwright-webui ein claude-review.yml das (a) anthropics direct via @anthropic-ai/claude-code npm package nutzt, (b) jeden PR reviewt ohne Tier-Filter, (c) auf 'develop' Branch triggert (existiert gar nicht). Migration auf OpenRouter + Custom-Script + Tier-Logik analog Monorepo B4.5 Phase 2. Branch (im WebUI Repo!): `iterate/automerge-pr-review-alignment`. Voraussetzung: Monorepo B4.5 Phase 2 (`trg-52cd3143`) merged — der etabliert das kanonische Pattern.
launchPayload:
/shipwright-iterate

Brief: Align shipwright-webui PR-Review architecture with monorepo B4.5 Phase 2 — replace claude-review.yml (anthropics-direct, always-on) with pr-review.yml (OpenRouter, tiered).

RUN THIS AGAINST shipwright-webui repo, NOT shipwright monorepo.

Files to touch in WebUI repo:
- .github/workflows/claude-review.yml: DELETE (replaced by pr-review.yml)
- .github/workflows/pr-review.yml: NEW, ported from monorepo B4.5 Phase 2 pattern:
  * Job-Name 'PR Review' (Branch-Protection-Required-Check match)
  * Two-Job-Struktur: decide (Tier-Filter) + review (gated)
  * Fork-PR-Guard: head.repo.full_name == github.repository
  * Tier 3 trigger: external author (NOT 'svroch' / 'dependabot[bot]') OR sensitive paths (client/**, server/**, .github/workflows/**) OR label 'needs-review'
  * Skip override: label 'skip-pr-review'
  * Trigger: branches: [main] only (NOT [main, develop] — develop existiert nicht)
- scripts/pr_review.py: NEW, WebUI-side adaption (~120-150 LOC):
  * Same JSON contract, same exit-code semantics als monorepo
  * OpenRouter call via $OPENROUTER_API_KEY
  * Default model 'anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6' via $SHIPWRIGHT_PR_REVIEW_MODEL
  * tsx + node available on ubuntu-latest runner — script kann pure-Python sein
- prompts/pr_reviewer/system/ + user/: NEW, WebUI-spezifische Heuristiken (Vite + Hono Stack, React-Components, Server-Routes, RLS, etc.) — analog zur monorepo system/user Verzeichnis-Form (PR #119 Pattern)
- Tests in tests/: pr_review_workflow_shape.test.ts + pr_review_script.test.ts (vitest)

Smoke-Tests vor merge:
1. Test-PR aus svroch Account (Tier 2) → review-Job skipped, Required-Check trotzdem grün
2. Test-PR mit Änderung in client/components/ → Tier 3 (sensitive path), Script reviewt

Nach merge (manuelle Sven-Aktion):
- OPENROUTER_API_KEY als Repo-Secret in shipwright-webui (separate Spend-Limit!)
- Branch Protection neu setzen mit 6 Required Checks:
    * Client (type + lint + test)
    * Server (type + lint + test)
    * Shipwright Security Scan
    * Analyze (javascript-typescript)
    * Anti-ratchet + allowlist diff
    * PR Review (NEW, ersetzt 'Claude Code Review')
- KEIN develop Branch in Protection target (existiert nicht, war dead config)
- Allow auto-merge wie monorepo

Constraint (defensiv):
- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY Secret im WebUI nach Migration NICHT löschen sofort — falls rollback gewünscht, ist es eine 1-Zeilen-Git-Revert. Erst nach 1-2 erfolgreichen Tier-3-Reviews löschen.

Complexity hint: small-medium (Port + Adapt, kein neues Architektur-Design)

title: Intelligent bloat gate: falsifiable reducibility reviewer (LOC becomes a router, not the verdict)
detail: Replace the raw >300-LOC verdict with an LLM reducibility reviewer. Cheap LOC signal becomes the ROUTER that escalates to the reviewer; the reviewer blocks only on a concrete, falsifiable reduction from a closed catalog (D duplication / A needless-abstraction / X dead-code / C control-flow / S data-shape / M comment-restating-code / P dependency-footprint / T test-repetition), each citing what-to-remove + est-LOC-saved + keeps-tests-green; no concrete finding -> PASS. Guardrails G1-G6: long-but-coherent is never a finding, clarity>cleverness, never weaken coverage/validation/types, no churn that breaks merge stability, justified duplication (G5) exempt, generated/vendored exempt. Two goals: (A) prevent over-production = blocking on the diff; (B) boy-scout improve-on-touch = advisory, bounded to the touched unit. Lands: catalog as shared reference + per-language idiom-map in shared/profiles/; reviewer dimension in plugins/shipwright-build/agents/code-reviewer.md + shared/prompts/iterate_reviewer; enforcement via review cascade (bounce-back) + optional CI tiered-review (B4.5). Dogfood (this iterate): catalog applied to 8 H1 files -> 7/8 correctly long-coherent (no forced churn), 1/8 a real D2 (see resolver-consolidation item).

title: Consolidate 3 repo-root resolvers into lib/repo_root.py (events_log.resolve_main_repo_root is in the wrong module)
detail: Three functions resolve the MAIN repo root via git rev-parse --git-common-dir: lib/worktree_isolation.main_repo_root (raises), lib/repo_root.main_repo_root_or (fail-soft fallback), lib/events_log.resolve_main_repo_root (returns None + warnings). The third is a repo-root primitive squatting in the event-log module -- its own docstring states it is 'no longer used to locate the event log'; its real consumers are the decision-drop resolvers (write_decision_drop, aggregate_decisions), the F11 verifier (iterate_checks), plugin_sync_reminder_on_stop, and compliance group_f. Proposed fix: move the implementation into lib/repo_root.py (its thematic home) and re-export from events_log for back-compat, then migrate call sites + tests. Constraints to respect: reverses the documented 2026-05-29 keep-in-place decision (rewrite the events_log docstring sections); ~10 consumer/test files; test_decision_drop_ssot.py greps the literal string 'resolve_main_repo_root' in consumer sources; dedicated tests in test_events_log.py / test_git_tools_utf8.py import it from events_log. Out of scope for a bloat cleanup; needs its own iterate. Surfaced by the H1 bloat dogfood on events_log.py (323 LOC).

reason: H/H1 resolved in this iterate: 8 oversize files grandfathered into shipwright_bloat_baseline.json (Group-H scan now H1=0). Rolling compliance-backlog item superseded; the producer's next run would emit the same complianceResolved.

title: RTM collector crashes on explicit-null affected_frs (no None guard)
detail: plugins/shipwright-compliance/scripts/lib/collectors/_types.py builds WorkEvent.affected_frs via d.get('affected_frs', []), whose default only applies when the key is ABSENT. A work_completed event carrying an explicit 'affected_frs': null (e.g. when finalize_iterate is given --event-extras-json with affected_frs:null, instead of the normal omit) yields None, and rtm.map_requirements_to_events then does 'for fr_id in we.affected_frs' -> TypeError: NoneType is not iterable, crashing the whole compliance markdown regen (dashboard/RTM/sbom/test-evidence/change-history). Latent: the normal finalize flow omits the key, so 5/6 recent events are fine; surfaced when this iterate passed explicit null. Fix: coerce null+missing alike, e.g. d.get('affected_frs') or [] (same for new_frs and any sibling list fields), with a regression test feeding an explicit-null event. Generalizes so any producer that emits null can't brick compliance.

title: Adopt: scaffold bloat-check.yml + vendor anti-ratchet runner (deferred from B4.6)
detail: Follow-up to the B4.6 Automerge-Readiness Pack (trg-a678bd00). The codeql.yml + AUTOMERGE_SETUP.md portion shipped in iterate-2026-06-13-adopt-automerge-readiness; bloat-check.yml was deferred because the monorepo workflow invokes shared/scripts/hooks/anti_ratchet_check.py which is absent in adopted repos (scaffolding it verbatim = a permanently-red Required Check). To close: ship an opt-in bloat-check that actually runs in an adopted repo. Options weighed: (A) vendor the minimal runner (anti_ratchet_check.py + lib/anti_ratchet.py + lib/bloat_baseline.py, ~590 LOC stdlib-only, into .shipwright/scripts/ with a source-hash drift marker like the webui vendored copy); (B) a self-contained single-file bloat-check (~90 LOC, duplicates ratchet logic = a 2nd drift surface). Adopt already generates shipwright_bloat_baseline.json (Step A.0), so only the runner + workflow are missing. Gate it behind a Step-C interview question (default no). Not required for automerge (AUTOMERGE_SETUP.md documents it as a manual opt-in).

title: Atomic config writers: add fsync for crash-durability (cross-cutting)
detail: External review of iterate-2026-06-13-runconfig-atomic-writes: run_config_store.atomic_write_json and the ~10 sibling _atomic_write helpers do tmp+os.replace WITHOUT fsync. Torn reads are prevented, but a crash/power-loss between write and replace can drop the latest content. Cross-cutting durability decision for all atomic writers; out of WP2 scope (concurrency, not durability).

title: update_step: pre-lock standalone read triggers an unlocked legacy migration
detail: Internal review of iterate-2026-06-13-runconfig-atomic-writes: update_step reads the 'standalone' flag via _load_or_bootstrap BEFORE acquiring run_config_lock; for a LEGACY config that load triggers an unlocked one-time migration write (legacy_migration -> save_run_config). Residual narrow F11-class window (legacy-only, idempotent, now-atomic). Fix: read the standalone flag without triggering the migration write, or determine is_standalone inside the lock.

title: Hook fan-out — redundant cross-cutting work ran N× per event; deduped via once-per-event guard + session-state phase resolver
detail: Claude Code fires every enabled plugin's hooks with no active-plugin filter, so each shared cross-cutting hook did its real work N× per event (SessionStart/Stop/PostToolUse ×11–12), and audit_phase_quality_on_stop keyed the audited phase off CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT → one Stop audited 11 phases, 10 of which never ran. RESOLVED in iterate-2026-06-14-hook-fanout-dedup (symmetric, no single controlling plugin): genuinely-redundant work is wrapped in a fail-open once-per-(event,session) guard (event_once.claim_once_for_event) on audit_phase_quality_on_stop + generate_handoff_on_stop + check_artifact_drift; the audit resolves which phase(s) ran from SESSION STATE (phase_quality.resolve_engaged_phases over events.jsonl + run_config) instead of plugin root. register-everywhere kept (test_hook_registry_bloat invariant + robustness across pipeline AND iterate). mark_plugin_edit/check_file_size were left unchanged (already convergent set-idempotent/upsert markers). Comprehensive integration-tests/test_hook_fanout_consolidation.py (exactly-once, phase-from-session-state, fail-open, robust-when-first-plugin-disabled, parallel-fan-out atomicity, marker convergence). Earlier SessionStart injection spam was already fixed via capture_session_id's claim_once. Supersedes the abandoned 2026-06-02-hook-consolidation campaign framing (single-owner-in-iterate).

reason: Implemented: symmetric once-per-event guard + session-state phase resolver; see iterate spec + integration-tests/test_hook_fanout_consolidation.py.

title: Consolidate divergent Shipwright-project detection predicates (audit-1 a1-2 follow-up)
detail: Three project-detection predicates coexist (lib.project_root._is_shipwright_project, phase_quality.is_shipwright_project, track_tool_calls._is_shipwright_project) with slightly different marker sets. a1-2's F7 drift guard correctly delegates to the project_root one, but a foreign tree carrying only one marker can be classified differently across hooks. Consolidate on one canonical predicate so all hooks agree on the greenfield/foreign boundary.

title: triage_gc: make under-lock GC recompute union-residence-aware (audit-1 a1-6 follow-up)
detail: a1-6/F19 closed the tracked-route TOCTOU (recompute droppable set under the lock), but the recompute reads the tracked store only. On idle-main-with-origin a concurrent re-open routes to the gitignored outbox and stays invisible to the recompute, so the item is still dropped and the outbox status orphans. Make the recompute consider union residence (still rewriting only the tracked file) and add an outbox-route survival test. Documented as an operator constraint in triage_gc.py until fixed. Also (low): make the reverse-drift meta-test source-derived rather than a hand-copied token set, and resolve the legacy auditResolved orphan token; extract the duplicated _strip_control_chars into shared/ and cover the GC dry-run report render surface.

title: Encoding/test-coverage follow-ups from audit-1 UTF-8 work (a1-3, a1-5)
detail: a1-5: align shared/scripts/lib/config.py read_config to encoding=utf-8-sig so BOM tolerance is consistent across all config readers (the four readers fixed in a1-5 use utf-8-sig; read_config still uses plain utf-8 and would JSONDecodeError on a hand-edited BOM config). a1-3: add direct tests for integrate_main's followup_commit_failed (exit 8) and merge_commit_failed_abort_incomplete (abort double-fault) branches — both newly introduced, currently uncovered (the blocked-merge-commit AC is covered).

title: Campaign independent-branch decision-drop bleed false-fails arch-md test locally
detail: In an autonomous campaign with independent branches run sequentially on one shared working tree, gitignored decision-drops accumulate across branches while each branch's architecture.md is committed per-branch. So test_architecture_md_reflects_arch_impact false-fails locally on a later branch (it sees a sibling's arch-impact decision-drop whose architecture.md entry lives only on the sibling's PR). It does NOT occur in CI (decision-drops are gitignored, never pushed). Consider scoping the test to the current run_id, or having campaign sub-iterates run in isolated worktrees so gitignored artifacts don't bleed.

title: Compress agent-doc backlog to one-line ADR pointers (Iterate 2/3 of agent-doc-entry-rules)
detail:
Follow-up to PR #206 (agent-doc-entry-rules Iterate 1: added the IMPACT_TARGETS routing SSoT, the 600-char forward-only entry-budget gate, and the F2/F3a/reflection one-line-pointer rules). Iterate 1 fixed the RULES; this is the 'compress the backlog' half.

SCOPE
- architecture.md '## Architecture Updates': ~50 verbose iterate-* entries -> one line each: '**<run_id>** (date): <impact> — <gist>. -> decision_log (Run-ID)'. (~30 ADR-NNN lines are already compact.)
- conventions.md '## Convention Updates' + '## Learnings': same treatment.

DETAIL PRESERVATION (decision): migrate each verbose original verbatim into an on-demand archive under .shipwright/planning/adr/ (one consolidated archive doc is fine) OR its existing ADR, THEN pointer-ize. Do NOT drop detail.

THEN (after convention entries are migrated out of '## Architecture Updates' into conventions.md '## Convention Updates'):
- retire the transitional _LEGACY_CONVENTION_DOC fallback in shared/scripts/lib/architecture_doc.py;
- lower the entry-budget gate _ENFORCED_FROM in plugins/shipwright-iterate/tests/test_agent_doc_entry_rules.py to enforce retroactively.

GATE-SAFE: compressed entries keep their run_ids (drift test + F11 check_architecture_documented pass) and keep dates < 2026-06-13 (grandfathered by the budget gate). Suggested reviewable split: Iterate 2 = architecture.md; Iterate 3 = conventions.md + fallback retirement + cutoff lowering.

A resumable empty worktree iterate/compress-architecture-updates was set up then abandoned; start fresh. Context: project memory project_agent_doc_entry_rules_split.

title: Auto-merge churn fix (Option A): serial integrate_main merge for campaign/parallel iterates
detail: GitHub auto-merge (PR #197) does a server-side merge and never runs integrate_main / resolve_churn_conflicts (the regenerate-at-merge resolver). For a single sequential iterate (branch current with main) auto-merge is fine, but for PARALLEL iterate branches (--autonomous campaign, or concurrent iterates) each branch commits its own regenerated derived snapshots (.shipwright/compliance/*.md, .shipwright/agent_docs/*.md, shipwright_test_results.json). As branches merge serially, every still-open branch goes DIRTY (auto-merge stalls) or merges stale (Group E staleness noise) because the regen step never runs server-side. events.jsonl + triage.jsonl are fine (merge=union); only the regenerate-on-conflict snapshots break. Seen live in campaign 2026-06-10-audit-1-auto: #199/#204/#205 merged, #200-#203 cascaded DIRTY/behind and needed manual integrate_main merges; an UNRELATED PR #206 (agent-doc compaction) re-conflicted them on conventions.md mid-drain. DECISION = Option A: do the merge through integrate_main, SERIALIZED, for campaigns/concurrency; keep auto-merge only for single iterates whose branch is current; add an F11 guard that runs integrate_main first if the branch is behind. Host-agnostic, reuses existing machinery, softens no gate. REJECTED: GitHub Action post-merge (host-specific); untracking snapshots (breaks audit_staleness); Option C derived-cache restructure (disproportionate, would have to extend to agent docs too). FOLLOW-UP (Option B, later): automate the serial drain as the gh-pr-ci watcher producer on the B4.5 roadmap. Full plan + acceptance criteria + reuse pointers: Spec/automerge-churn-serial-integrate-plan.md. DO BEFORE the next autonomous campaign.

title: update-marketplace.sh F37 python probe picks the broken Windows python3 Store-stub (regression from #205)
detail: a1-7/F37 (#205) changed update-marketplace.sh to probe 'python3 -> python -> py' via 'command -v'. On Windows, python3 is usually the Microsoft Store App-Execution-Alias STUB: command -v finds it, but running it prints 'Python was not found; install from the Microsoft Store' and exits 49. Under set -euo pipefail the first cache_target=$(python3 -c ...) then aborts the whole script right after 'Full sync:' with exit 49 -> the plugin-cache sync silently does nothing on Windows (plugin fixes never reach the runtime cache; the campaign's own merged plugin changes did not propagate until worked around). F37 fixed the POSIX case (bare python absent) but introduced a Windows regression. FIX: probe by TEST-RUNNING each candidate, not just command -v: 'if command -v "$c" >/dev/null 2>&1 && "$c" --version >/dev/null 2>&1; then PYTHON_BIN=$c; break; fi'. The stub fails --version (49) and the probe falls through to the real 'python'. Validated: with the test-run probe the full sync completes (13 synced, 0 errors) on this Windows box. File: scripts/update-marketplace.sh (probe lines ~40-45). Same test-run pattern likely wanted wherever else the python3-first command-v probe was copied (e.g. verify-setup.sh). Add a shell test that simulates a python3 that exits non-zero on --version and asserts the probe picks the next candidate.

reason: P5.1 obsolete: MU4 (optimistic triage mutations) no longer fits the rebuilt modal-based Triage/Status architecture - actions live in TriageDetailModal (no card-fade), Promote/Fix-now already open instantly, and it would need a new toast primitive for marginal gain. MU6 (self-hosting docs) already completed directly in guide.md. User decision 2026-06-13.
Don't need it. It is only affecting the transcript. The 1s is no issue there.

Multica-Domaenen-Mismatch: Multi-Workspace ist ein Team-/Multi-Org-SaaS-Konzept; Shipwright-WebUI ist ein lokales Single-User-Cockpit. Der flache Projekt-Board deckt die Zielgruppe (Solo-Dev/Founder/kleine Boutique) bereits ab. YAGNI vor Public-Launch; bei echtem Gruppierungsbedarf reicht ein Tag/Filter statt voller Workspace-Ebene + Migration.

Bundle nicht jetzt: MU-PL2 (AST-Boundary-Check) ist praeventiv ohne realen Crosstalk-Vorfall - shared/contracts/ (B8) + etablierte Konvention decken den Pfad bereits. MU-PL3 (parse-don-t-cast) adressiert eine echte Fragilitaet, aber das Prinzip lebt schon ad-hoc als defensive Guards (vgl. WorkEvent.from_dict null-coercion). Volle Pydantic-Migration aller Configs ist zu grosser Lift fuer P3. Bei echtem Plugin-Crosstalk/Config-Drift-Schmerz einzeln wieder aufmachen.

Research-Sprint mit faktisch bereits getroffenen Verdikten: MU7 (Daemon) sagt in der eigenen Spec woertlich -nicht jetzt-; MU-PL1 (skills-lock) setzt ein Drittanbieter-Plugin-Oekosystem voraus, das vor Public-Launch nicht existiert; MU5 (Internal-Packages-Monorepo) ist Over-Engineering fuer ein 2-Workspace-WebUI ohne realen Typ-Duplikations-Schmerz. ~1.5 Tage fuer drei -nicht-jetzt--ADRs = niedrige Rendite. Jede Frage einzeln wieder aufmachen, wenn der jeweilige Trigger feuert.

title: Campaign: Compliance Control-Grade coverage (BP-1/BP-2 + remaining AR; AR-1-3 run parallel)
detail: Anchor for the baseline campaign delivering the compliance Control-Grade 'proof asset' (public grader + certification lead magnet). Scope: BP-2 (persist per-FR behavior-affecting impact onto the event + reader parses it), BP-1 finish (traced-% dashboard metric + behavior-aware fail-closed verifier + backfill of untagged events; the FR-or-no-FR gate core already shipped via ADR-059), AR-10 MUST (surface CI security gate results in the dashboard), AR-04 P0 (SBOM dedupe/license/honest-summary), and AR-05 (RTM Reconciled? column consuming the new impact field; coordinate with parallel AR-01). AR-01/02/03 run separately in the iterate/compliance-control-grade worktree. Forward goal: the Control Grade must eventually grade an arbitrary repo (repo-agnostic signal interface), not just a Shipwright repo - an in-flight research session may reshape this baseline. Source: Spec/course-launch-compliance-control-coverage.md (BP-1, BP-2, AR-04, AR-05, AR-10).

title: check_security_scan PreToolUse gate fails closed on hook error, blocking unrelated work
detail: The check_security_scan Bash PreToolUse hook intermittently errors ('No stderr output') and fail-closes, blocking ALL Bash calls (git add, test runs, file reads), not just commit/push, even during a findings-REDUCING change. Observed repeatedly in iterate-2026-06-27-codeql-security-hardening; retries usually clear it (flakiness, not deterministic policy). Proposed: (a) scope the gate to commit/push intents via argv/intent rather than every Bash call; (b) fail-OPEN with a logged warning when the hook itself errors (a crashing check should not hard-block work); (c) add a remediation/override-aware path. Workaround today: user approval + override logged to compliance_overrides.log + retry.

title: Review the genuine CodeQL quality remainder after PR #276 merges
detail: After PR #276 merges and CodeQL re-runs with the tailored config, the 153 open alerts drop to ~11 genuine quality findings kept armed: 6 py/cyclic-import (non-registration modules, left open unverified), 3 py/implicit-string-concatenation-in-list (can be real missing-comma bugs), 2 py/mixed-returns. Action: verify the 6 cyclic-imports, inspect the 3 implicit-string-concat, accept/dismiss mixed-returns. The other 142 self-resolve (22 fixed-in-PR on merge; 120 quality-noise once the new query-filter config is active) and must NOT be manually dismissed.

title: BP-1 (WebUI): backfill change-to-requirement linkage; start with a Compliance refresh to set the baseline
detail: WebUI-repo data side of BP-1 (the code/mechanism half ships shared from the monorepo plugins -- no separate WebUI code). Start with a /shipwright-compliance refresh in the shipwright-webui repo (now-updated shared plugin) to regenerate dashboard.md + RTM and set the real traced-percent baseline. Then backfill: tag the WebUI's untagged work_completed events to FRs (or an explicit no_fr_reason) and close its NOT-VERIFIED requirements so the RTM unfreezes and the Control Grade is honest. Note: BP-2 (persist per-FR impact on the event) is the mechanism and is monorepo-only/shared -- no WebUI work. Runs as iterates IN the shipwright-webui repo.

reason: Removed the noise with a change of the mechanics with PR #287

title: Recalibrate compliance metrics that count legitimately-exempt items as deficits (bloat / iterate-tests / audit-suggestions / audit-staleness)
detail: Compliance producers flag legitimately-exempt items as deficits -> inflated WARNs / alarm-fatigue (same root flaw BP-1 traceability-credit fixed). Surfaced on the shipwright-webui A99 dashboard; the LOGIC is monorepo (compliance-plugin collectors). FINDINGS: (1) Dashboard "Bloat over-limit: 80" counts grandfathered files (82/85 baseline entries) as "not ADR-justified" -- grandfathering IS the acceptance; real signal is the ratchet (PASS, -541, shrinking). Key the WARN off ratchet-regressions / new-crossings; downgrade the grandfathered count to INFO. (2) Dashboard + test-evidence.md "Iterate tests passing: N without tests" counts merges (source=backfill-merge-retro), docs, B7-backfill, compliance-refreshes and none_reason events -- all legitimately test-free. Exclude non-feature events by source/change_type (mirror the BP-1 traced-credit). (3) audit-report.md C1/C2/C3 PASSING ("trivially satisfied") checks still emit a "Suggested: /shipwright-iterate ... reconcile" remediation line -> false action on a non-problem; gate suggestions on real FAIL only. (4) audit-report.md is NOT regenerated by update_compliance --phase iterate (only by /shipwright-compliance), so on-disk it can show month-old findings (here 2026-05-22; 4x F-FAIL possibly already resolved) -- add a staleness marker or refresh it in the routine regen. CLEAN (no change): SBOM (AR-04 fixed), RTM (age-neutral + Reconciled), change-history. Likely fix files: shipwright-compliance/scripts/lib/_bloat_dashboard_rows.py + the iterate-tests row in collectors/dashboard.py; the audit suggestion-gating in run_audit + a staleness marker.

reason: NOT pinning GitHub-owned actions (decided 2026-06-30, risk-driven, not to-be-green): both repos already SHA-pin all third-party actions (the high-risk tag-hijack class); the remainder is GitHub-owned = Scorecard discounted 20% slice (weight 8:2), lowest-prob compromise, and clean pinning would need a Dependabot updater (SHA-rot) conflicting with the no-Dependabot stance. Squash-only already enforced repo-level; webui ruleset aligned strict=true. See memory project_actions_pinning_decision.

title: Decompose coarse one-FR-per-plugin requirements into sub-FRs (esp. FR-01.10 compliance)
detail: The 14 FRs are one-per-plugin/command (FR-01.10 = the whole /shipwright-compliance plugin), so coverage is trivially 14/14 and never moves, and real capabilities added inside a plugin (Control Grade, honesty gate, CI-security ingestion, SBOM quality, RTM reconciliation) have no specific FR to map to -> they get filed no-FR, depressing strict FR-traceability. Decompose the fat plugin-FRs into sub-FRs (e.g. FR-01.10.x: Control Grade / RTM / SBOM / CI-security ingestion / honesty gate; similarly FR-01.13 adopt, FR-01.11 iterate, FR-01.07 security). Product-modeling task: needs owner input on granularity. Makes coverage meaningful + the grade reflect the real feature surface. Paired with the re-tag follow-up (fr-model-followup).

title: Re-tag mis-filed compliance/security FEATURE events to their parent FRs (clears the traceability decline)
detail: Recent /shipwright-compliance + /shipwright-security FEATURE work was filed change_type=compliance/no-FR though it delivers FR-01.10 (and FR-01.07) functionality, depressing strict FR-tagging to ~10% (vs 18% all-time) -> honesty-gate caps the grade at B. Fix: append event_amended overlays (record_event.py --type event_amended --amends <id>) setting affected_frs + spec_impact=modify on the genuine features. RE-TAG -> FR-01.10 (security ones +FR-01.07): Goodhart Control Grade/honesty gate; AR-10 CI-security ingestion; AR-10 SARIF fallback; AR-04 SBOM quality; AR-05 RTM Reconciled column; Control-Grade anchors/maintainability anchor. STAY no-FR (real infra): scorecard add/fix/remove (CI), mtime drift-detector removal, bloat-baseline tighten, events_log lazy-import doc, ci-security.json data refresh. CAVEAT: spec_impact=modify makes FR-01.10/FR-01.07 behavior-touched -> VERIFY the reconciliation dimension stays green (each touched FR needs a test run at/after its last touch; link a later test event or don't tag the very latest as modify). Expected: recent 3/30 -> ~9/30, decline clears, grade -> A honestly. Pairs with the sub-FR decomposition (fr-model-followup); ideally do the decomposition first so features map to specific sub-FRs.

title: Measure patch/diff coverage (were the CHANGED lines tested?), not just pass-rate
detail: Reviewer comment B: '3618/3618 green' is pass-rate, not coverage — it says nothing about whether AI-added code is even executed. Killer case for AI-first: AI writes code + a trivial test that misses the risky branch; pass-rate stays green, new code untested. The Test Completeness Ledger is an author-CLAIMED checklist, not instrumented. Today CI runs plain pytest (no --cov). BUILD: pytest --cov -> coverage.xml -> diff-cover against the PR merge-base = % of CHANGED lines covered; surface as (a) a CI gate (warn/fail under threshold) AND (b) an input to the Control Grade Test-health dimension (note: the W4 verifier already reads shipwright_test_results.json coverage.total but the field is never populated -> SKIP). SCOPE/caveats: touches CI across ALL plugins (each has its own pytest step) + a coverage-combine step + a schema field + grade wiring -> its own iterate (biggest of the A/B/C/D set, why it wasn't bundled into #296). Generic repo-grader story (arbitrary languages) is harder -> do monorepo dogfood first, defer the generic case. Central to the 'control over AI code' thesis.

title: github_triage artifact-ingest counts inline-suppressed Semgrep findings as live, inflating gh-security triage and blocking auto-resolve
detail: shared/scripts/github_triage/producer.py security_artifact_action_unit() sets artifact_total = len(findings) with no suppression filter. On the artifact-ingest path (GHAS Code Scanning unavailable, cs_alerts is None) this counts inline-suppressed Semgrep findings (SARIF results carrying suppressions:[{kind:inSource}]) as live, inflating the gh-security:<owner>/<repo> action-unit count. Since the count never reaches 0, resolve_stale never auto-dismisses the item and it re-appears after every manual dismiss. Fix: count only results where (.suppressions // []) | length == 0 before len(), so a fully-suppressed scan yields 0 findings -> producer returns None -> auto-resolve gate fires. Add a regression test with a SARIF fixture containing suppressed results. Found during a shipwright-webui security review of CI run 28407935303 (12 inline-suppressed FPs surfaced as 12 low).

title: Decompose FR-01.10 / FR-01.07 into sub-FRs for precise feature traceability
detail: Follow-up to iterate-2026-06-30-fr-retag-honesty. Introduce sub-FRs (e.g. FR-01.10.x for Control Grade / RTM / SBOM / detective; FR-01.07.x for the scanner chain) so individual compliance and security features map to specific sub-requirements instead of the broad parent FR, then re-tag the overlaid features onto their sub-FRs. Separate spec-ADD iterate.

reason: Implemented on both repos, Shipwright #299 and WebUI #191

title: pr_review Tier-3 truncates on medium+ iterates: exclude generated artifacts from the review diff (82% of tokens are derived)
detail: pr_review.py Tier-3 review truncates + fails-closed on medium+ iterates because it sends the FULL gh-pr-diff (no path filter) and generated artifacts dominate the token budget. Measured on PR #310: total diff 295,497 chars (~74k tok) vs MAX_DIFF_CHARS=200,000 -> truncated. Breakdown: DERIVED artifacts 241,286 chars (82%) — test-evidence.md alone 119,670, uv.lock 77,758 — vs real SOURCE only 54,211 chars (~13.6k tok, well under cap). So every medium+ iterate (regenerates compliance MDs + bumps uv.lock) trips truncation, and the skip-pr-review workaround then DISABLES review entirely on exactly the large PRs that need it. The fail-closed-on-truncation itself is correct design (#263); the fixable part is the INPUT bloat. FIX (own iterate, --type change): in pr_review.py filter the unified diff to DROP file-sections for generated paths (.shipwright/compliance/, .shipwright/agent_docs/, *.lock, shipwright_test_results.json, shipwright_events.jsonl, CHANGELOG-unreleased.d/) and record the excluded list in the PR meta (transparent, not silent). Effect: -82% tokens -> review runs + the model sees only real code (better quality). REJECTED: bumping MAX_DIFF_CHARS (symptom-only, wastes tokens on lockfiles). CAVEATS: pr_review.py is in plugins/shipwright-security (sensitive Tier-3a path -> own PR); WebUI has a VENDORED copy that needs a separate webui iterate to stay in parity; keep the exclusion list conservative + logged. expands_triage this id; dismiss after fix.

title: Harden diff-coverage CI gate: prove the fail-path (integration test) before the hard-flip
detail: Phase 4 (#324) shipped the warn-only diff-cover --fail-under=80 CI gate, but its FAIL-path has never actually run: PR #324's own CI reported 'No lines with coverage information in this diff', so the threshold was a no-op. The gate is only string-asserted; the inline shell wrapper (rc-capture / report-print / exit) is unproven. Before flipping the gate to hard-block, prove it bites: (1) move the gate decision into a testable measure_diff_coverage.py --fail-under entrypoint that ci.yml calls; (2) integration-test the fail-path (undercovered changed line -> exit != 0 + report printed; covered -> exit 0); (3) pin diff-cover + migrate the deprecated --json-report/--markdown-report flags to --format. Small 'change' iterate, still warn-only; prerequisite to the Phase-4 hard-flip. Full spec (gitignored, local): .shipwright/planning/iterate/campaigns/diff-coverage/sub-iterates/5-gate-hardening.md -- Start with: /shipwright-iterate on that spec path.

title: shipwright-grade G6: calibrate cold-repo projection heuristics (real repos mis-grade F, ordering inverted)
detail: G5's empirical launch gate is RED: the cold-repo projector mis-grades well-run repos. Evidence (5 real repos): pallets/flask F 19.9 (below deprecated request/request 41.2 - ordering inverted); express F 49.0; obra/superpowers F 46.7; addyosmani/agent-skills F 49.0. Two projection-heuristic root causes (NOT the shared scorer): (1) change_traceability is systematically ~0.18 (flask 24/500, superpowers 92/500, agent-skills 37/209) vs express 0.65 - the is_traced classifier under-credits most commit styles; (2) the network test-health tier is unreliable across repos (agent-skills 1.00 fired, superpowers n/a did-not-fire, flask 0.00 wrong-read). G6 tunes the projection heuristics ONLY, with a dogfood-A regression guard, then re-refreshes fixtures so the gate goes green. Spec: campaigns/2026-07-03-shipwright-grade/sub-iterates/G6-projector-calibration.md. Verification: re-grade all manifest repos (incl. superpowers + agent-skills) at the end of G6.

reason: Delivered: the fail-path is proven by the tested measure_diff_coverage.py --fail-under entrypoint + a real synthetic-repo integration test (iterate-2026-07-06-diff-coverage-gate-hardening) and a real-PR replay corpus (iterate-2026-07-06-diff-coverage-real-pr-replay); the hard flip now lands on proven teeth.

Campaign complete: diff-coverage Phases 1-4 delivered, gate hardened + real-PR replay evidence gathered, and the hard flip (iterate-2026-07-06-diff-coverage-hard-flip) makes a PR with < 80% changed-line coverage BLOCK merge.

WebUI work: a Review-mockups button on the paused design card that opens the viewer inline, writes .shipwright/designs/design-feedback-round{N}.md straight to the worktree on submit (no manual export), plus a Resume action that clears the gate. Monorepo side already supports it (Option-B reader + the feedback-file convention). Open decisions: gitignore the feedback rounds as transient scratch; emit structured per-screen feedback. WebUI-track follow-up.

title: Remove the deprecated multi-session pipeline engine (single-session is now the sole mode)
detail: Decision 2026-07-08 (Sven): single-session is the sole pipeline mode; multi-session no longer needed (one user, no back-compat). Migration verified trivial: set run_config.mode to single_session + resume the pipeline (phase_tasks are shared; re-claim is idempotent). Deferred cleanup: mark multi-session deprecated, then remove the external per-phase-session engine + per-phase Continue. Do AFTER single-session is battle-tested (post AC5 + the webui-pipeline-convergence campaign). Load-bearing cross-plugin hooks -> own effort, explicitly NOT part of that campaign (Sven).

title: CLAUDE.md content ungoverned: agents inline full ADR rationale instead of a terse index
detail: agent_doc_budget.py (600 chars/entry) governs architecture.md + conventions.md entries but NOT CLAUDE.md; claude-md-template.md seeds only a small Gotchas section (no DO-NOT / Architecture-rules block) and gives no guidance on WHERE invariants belong. Result: adopted repos accrete large numbered DO-NOT/architecture-rule blocks that inline full ADR rationale (webui CLAUDE.md ~2.4x the monorepo's; its DO-NOT block alone is ~46% of the file). Proposed framework fix: writing-guidance + template note that CLAUDE.md = orientation + terse invariant index (one line + ADR pointer), full rationale in the ADRs it already cites; optionally extend a per-section budget/ratchet to CLAUDE.md. Prevents recurrence in all adopted repos (webui, leadwright, future). Pairs with the webui slim-CLAUDE.md item.

title: Per-split phase-duration accuracy: multi-split pipeline phases (build/plan) undercount in the tracked log
detail: B1 emits N phase_started (one per split phase_task, not deduped) but record_event dedups phase_completed by phase (first-wins), so a multi-split pipeline phase (build/plan fan out one phase_task per split, same phase) keeps only the FIRST split's phase_completed in the tracked shipwright_events.jsonl. The tracked-log start+end pair therefore reflects the FIRST split and the per-phase duration UNDERCOUNTS; single-split phases are exact. Decide the accuracy fix: per-split duration bars (widen the phase_completed dedup key to (phase, splitId)) vs. one phase_completed only at final-split completion. See campaign monorepo-wow-usability-2026-07-10 sub-iterate B1.

title: Cross-repo contract: the WebUI now renders grade's ReportModel + adopt's snapshot.json - say so in the SKILLs
detail:
The WebUI Command Center now RENDERS these two artifacts natively (WOW campaign
webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10, sub-iterate A08 "intent-wizard", triage trg-3edbc478). That turns
them from "our own output format" into a CROSS-REPO CONTRACT — and nothing in this repo says so.

WHAT THE WEBUI CONSUMES
  1. shipwright-grade: the `ReportModel` / `DimensionView` dataclasses
     (plugins/shipwright-grade/scripts/lib/report_model.py, rendered by html_report.py).
     The WebUI's "Grade your repo" screen renders THIS model field-for-field, deliberately, so
     the screen and the downloadable HTML report cannot tell different stories.
     Load-bearing fields it depends on:
       - dimensions[].status (ok|gap|n/a) -- drives the VISUAL. An n/a is drawn as absent
         evidence, never as a zero-score bar. The WebUI must never synthesize a score.
       - dimensions[].provenance + .detail -- the per-row "how this was measured" disclosure.
       - network_enabled / network_note / network_enrichments -- "exactly what left the
         machine". This is the receipt for the product's "read-only, no account" promise and
         is shown verbatim. If this field disappears, the WebUI loses its trust surface.
       - honest_ceiling_note, measurable_count, na_count, controls_shipwright_would_light[].
  2. shipwright-adopt: `.shipwright/adopt/snapshot.json` (stack, profile, conventions,
     test_frameworks, ci_pipeline, folders, nested_projects, features, git, commands) -- the
     WebUI's adopt screen shows "what's already here" from it before anything is written.

THE RISK
A field renamed or dropped here does not fail loudly in the WebUI -- it renders a half-empty
card or, worse, a plausible-but-wrong one. Nobody in this repo currently has a reason to know
the WebUI is watching.

TASK
  - State the contract in the SKILL.md of shipwright-grade and shipwright-adopt: these output
    shapes have an external consumer (the Command Center); a change to them requires a
    corresponding WebUI change. Point at the consumer.
  - Consider a versioned//stable marker on ReportModel (e.g. a schema version) so the WebUI can
    detect an unknown shape and say so honestly instead of rendering nonsense.
  - Also worth noting: grade.py accepts a local path OR a remote (shallow-cloned into a
    throwaway tempdir, purged after; --no-clone opts out). The WebUI surfaces the clone step
    and the network cost explicitly -- keep that behaviour stable or tell the WebUI.

WebUI side is already guarded: A08's spec requires payload-shape validation + an honest
"report shape not recognised" state rather than a half-empty render.

title: phase_timings rides along with the next iterate (verify it fires); phase_started needs a pipeline run, not an iterate
detail:
Producer gap found by the WebUI backend-completeness audit (2026-07-13): the event log has ZERO
live rows for phase_started / phase_timings.

=== CORRECTION (supersedes trg-eb989815, which conflated two mechanisms) ===
The old item said "fire ONE real iterate run ... verify the event log contains phase_started +
phase_timings". That is WRONG: an iterate will NEVER emit phase_started. Verified in the source --
these are two DIFFERENT producers:

  phase_timings  <- shared/scripts/tools/finalize_iterate.py + iterate_phase_timing.py
                    THE ITERATE FLOW. The 5 boundary marks (scope|build|review|test|finalize) are
                    emitted as the skill crosses each group boundary, and F5b folds the durations
                    into `work_completed.phase_timings`.
                    => ANY real iterate in this repo produces it. It is FREE.

  phase_started  <- plugins/shipwright-run/scripts/lib/orchestrator_pkg/{events,single_session_cli}.py
                    THE PIPELINE ORCHESTRATOR ONLY. Paired phase_started/phase_completed.
                    => needs a real /shipwright-run pipeline. An iterate cannot produce it.

(Reference: plugins/shipwright-iterate/skills/iterate/references/phase-timing.md, which states it
explicitly: the pipeline rail uses paired phase_started/phase_completed; the ITERATE flow instead
emits lightweight marks folded into work_completed.phase_timings.)

=== WHAT TO ACTUALLY DO ===
1. phase_timings — DO NOT schedule a dedicated run for this. Ride along with the next real iterate
   in this repo (e.g. the cross-repo-contract item trg-46a8de1f). Its own F5b emits the rows.
   The only work is to VERIFY afterwards that `work_completed.phase_timings` is present and
   populated in the event log. If it is NOT, then the emitter is genuinely broken (the marks are
   "additive and best-effort", so a skipped mark silently omits a bar) -- and THAT is the bug to
   fix. Confirming the emitter actually fires is the whole point.
2. phase_started — needs no dedicated task at all. It appears on the next real /shipwright-run
   pipeline. Do not run a full pipeline just to produce telemetry.
3. Optional (unchanged): an explicit gate-verdict object -- today the verdict is inferred rather
   than stated.

=== WHY IT MATTERS ===
The WebUI WOW campaign (webui-wow-usability-2026-07-10, A01/A02 = event-log reader) renders
per-phase durations from these rows. Until they exist the reader degrades to an honest `n/a`
(specified) -- it must NEVER fabricate or interpolate a duration.

title: Retarget the 8 phase-skill Step-0 blocks off the deleted SHIPWRIGHT-PIPELINE-CONTEXT block
detail:
The 8 phase skills still gate their Step-0 context recovery on the presence of a SHIPWRIGHT-PIPELINE-CONTEXT block ("if the PIPELINE-CONTEXT block is present, parse phaseTaskId and run get_phase_context.py; otherwise standalone"). The sole producer of that block was the phase_session_start SessionStart hook, deleted with the multi-session engine in iterate-2026-07-14-remove-multi-session. The trigger can therefore never fire, and every phase skill silently takes its standalone branch.

Not a regression from that iterate: single_session has been the default since SS8, and the phase runner is a SUBAGENT of the master, so it never had a bound Claude session whose id could match a phase_tasks[].sessionUuid — the block already never reached it. The removal only made the dead consumer visible.

Already closed the loop on the tool itself: the master now passes dispatch.phaseTaskId to the phase-runner, whose brief (agents/phase-runner.md Step 0) calls get_phase_context.py --phase-task-id directly. So prior-phase artifacts DO get loaded in the surviving mode.

Remaining work: retarget the 8 phase-skill Step-0 blocks (project, design, plan, build, test, security, changelog, deploy + their references/step-0-*.md) off the deleted context block and onto "the orchestrator handed you a phaseTaskId", so a phase skill invoked as a phase-runner subagent takes the pipeline branch rather than the standalone one. Also decide whether the Step-0 blocks are still needed at all now that the phase-runner brief does it.

reason: Fixed in this iterate instead of deferred. All three reviewers (internal adversarial, GPT-5.6, Codex) independently flagged the stale Step-0 trigger, so it was closed rather than carried: the 11 Step-0 blocks across the 8 phase skills now key on 'the orchestrator handed you a phaseTaskId' instead of the deleted SHIPWRIGHT-PIPELINE-CONTEXT block, matching the phase-runner brief. A new residue guard (test_no_shipped_skill_gates_on_the_deleted_context_block) keeps the dead trigger from returning.

title: Phase skills detect invocation mode from the unmaintained v1 current_step, so a driven phase can wrongly conclude standalone
detail:
The 8 phase skills classify "pipeline vs standalone" in their First-Actions step C ("Detect Invocation Mode") by reading shipwright_run_config.json status + current_step. Under the v2 pipeline those two fields are never advanced, so the check is blind for every driven run.

Empirically reproduced: drive project + design through the real single-session loop, then read the config. The phase_tasks frontier is correctly at "plan", but current_step is still "project" and completed_steps is []. grep confirms phase_task_lifecycle never writes current_step.

NOT a regression from iterate-2026-07-14-remove-multi-session, and this is the load-bearing point: the deleted phase_session_stop hook never wrote current_step either (verified against origin/main), so the check was equally blind under multi_session. It is the long-standing v1-compat vs v2 divergence — current_step/completed_steps are the v1 fields, phase_tasks is the v2 authority — surfaced by a reviewer looking at the phase skills with fresh eyes.

Consequence today: a phase skill dispatched by the orchestrator can conclude "standalone" from step C, skip its pipeline-state updates, and mark its own artifacts with mode: standalone, even though it is part of a driven run. Step 0 (context recovery) is unaffected — that was retargeted onto the phaseTaskId the orchestrator hands the phase-runner, and is guarded in both directions.

Fix direction: make step C key on the same thing Step 0 now does — the orchestrator handed you a phaseTaskId — or derive the invocation mode from phase_tasks rather than the unmaintained v1 fields. Touches all 8 phase skills plus their first-actions references, so it wants its own iterate rather than being bolted onto the removal PR.

Found by the Codex review of PR #369 (delta round).

title: Sweep quarantines a dismiss forever when the append lives only in main's tracked working tree (silent data loss + resurrection loop)
detail:
Reproduced live in shipwright-webui on 2026-07-14: triage item trg-6db81c59 kept
resurfacing on the board after every dismiss. Two defects compound into a loop that
SILENTLY EATS operator dismisses. The quarantine mechanism (#303) converted a loud
hard-block into quiet data loss.

== Evidence (shipwright-webui, 2026-07-14) ==
The `append` for trg-6db81c59 (and trg-6e048260) existed in NO git object:
  origin/main:.shipwright/triage.jsonl  -> 0 hits
  HEAD:.shipwright/triage.jsonl         -> 0 hits
  .shipwright/triage.outbox.jsonl       -> 0 hits
It existed ONLY as an uncommitted modification of main's TRACKED triage.jsonl
(`git status` showed `M .shipwright/triage.jsonl`, +2 lines).

triage.outbox.quarantine.jsonl recorded the same dismiss eaten repeatedly:
  2026-07-14T14:55:07  trg-6db81c59  dismissed  by=operatorReview
  2026-07-14T15:43:52  trg-6db81c59  dismissed  by=webui
  (trg-6e048260: 4x, from 2026-07-14T06:50:55 onward)
reason: "orphan-status: no append anywhere in the combined triage log"

== Defect A: a tracked-on-main append is delivered by NOTHING ==
`sweep_outbox_to_branch()` folds ONLY the gitignored outbox into the iterate branch.
An append that lands in main's TRACKED triage.jsonl (uncommitted) is picked up by no
producer, no sweep and no reconcile -- it sits in the working tree forever, invisible
to origin and to every worktree. The known rule "a tracked write on main = undelivered
drift" has no enforcement and no repair path. Whatever wrote those two appends bypassed
`should_route_to_outbox()`; the sweep should at minimum not let the drift rot silently.

== Defect B: the orphan check's universe is too small ==
`sweep_quarantine.decide(worktree_lines, outbox_lines, eol)` classifies over
`worktree-tracked (branched from origin/main) INTERSECT/UNION outbox` only. It never sees
MAIN's tracked log -- even though `sweep_outbox_to_branch()` already holds `main_root`.
So any status event for a drift-only append MUST look like an orphan. It is then
quarantined AND deleted from the outbox (sweep_outbox.py, the `if gc_dropped or
quarantined:` rewrite), and the sweep reports success. The operator's dismiss is gone.

Resurrection loop: the WebUI reader unions local-tracked (WITH the drift append) u origin
u outbox. Append present, dismiss quarantined away -> status falls back to `triage` ->
the item reappears -> operator dismisses again -> next sweep eats it again. Forever.

== Proposed fix ==
1. Widen the orphan universe: pass main_root's tracked log into `decide()` as a
   third read-only "known appends" source, so a status is only an orphan when its
   append exists in NEITHER worktree-tracked NOR outbox NOR main-tracked.
2. Do not silently drop a quarantined STATUS. Either keep it in the outbox until its
   append is origin-delivered, or surface the quarantine count loudly at setup (today
   `SweepResult.quarantined` is returned but the operator never sees it).
3. Detect + repair Defect A: if main's tracked log carries append lines absent from
   HEAD, the sweep should route them into the outbox (the real delivery channel)
   instead of leaving undelivered drift -- that is exactly the manual repair that
   fixed webui.

== Manual repair applied to shipwright-webui (reference implementation) ==
Moved both drift appends OUT of main's tracked log INTO the outbox, replayed the newest
quarantined dismiss behind them, reset the tracked log to HEAD. `sweep_quarantine.decide()`
then returns `clean` against origin/main, and the webui reader resolves both items to
`dismissed`. Regression tests should cover exactly this shape.

== Regression tests to add ==
- decide(): a status whose append exists ONLY in main-tracked is NOT an orphan.
- sweep: a drift append in main-tracked is routed to the outbox, not left behind.
- sweep: quarantining a status never silently removes the operator's only dismiss.

Reviewed across 4 rounds by Codex + GPT-5.6 + Gemini 3.1 Pro.

title: Retire the write-once v1 run-config fields (current_step / completed_steps)
detail: Follow-up from iterate-2026-07-14-phase-invocation-mode (external plan review, Gemini #2). The v2 lifecycle never advances current_step/completed_steps; config_factory stamps them once at run creation. Now that no phase skill reads them for invocation mode, they are write-once fields with only two remaining readers: phase_quality._resolution.resolve_source (keys on their PRESENCE for audit-source telemetry) and compliance mermaid.py (renders the dashboard phase strip). Deleting the writer without migrating those two would silently flip every driven run's audit source to standalone and blank the dashboard phase strip, so it was correctly out of scope. Scope for the follow-up: migrate both readers onto phase_tasks[] (the v2 authority), then drop the fields from config_factory + the schema. Deliberately NOT bundled with the invocation-mode fix (different blast radius).

title: shipwright-test suite leaks tests/fixtures/.shipwright/ into the working tree
detail: Pre-existing test-hygiene leak, NOT caused by the F0 parallel runner: running 'pytest tests/' in plugins/shipwright-test ALONE and SERIALLY also creates the untracked dir plugins/shipwright-test/tests/fixtures/.shipwright/. A test writes into its own fixtures dir instead of tmp_path, so every F0 run leaves the tree dirty. Fix: point the writing test at tmp_path (or clean up in a fixture teardown). Found during iterate-2026-07-14-f0-parallel-suite; deliberately NOT fixed there (surgical-changes rule: it is another plugin's test).

title: Iterate 2: bundle F1-F5c finalization into fewer LLM round-trips
detail: Iterate-duration campaign, part 2 of 3 (part 1 = F0 parallel runner, DELIVERED #371). The finalize phase is ~4.1 min in phase_timings telemetry, slow from ~8 SEQUENTIAL LLM round-trips (F1,F2,F3,F3a,F4,F5,F5b,F5c), NOT slow scripts (compliance regen is capped at 30s). Plan: a finalize_bundle.py that takes one JSON payload and calls the existing finalization tools UNCHANGED in dependency order (F1/F3/F3a/F4/F5/F5c independent -> F5b after F5 -> F6 last), reporting per-step and aborting with the failed step's name. ~4.1min -> ~3min. Full plan + dependency DAG + landmines in Claude memory project_finalization_bundling_plan. LANDMINE: touches the finalization machinery every iterate depends on -> cross_component risk flag -> needs integration coverage; highest blast radius of the 3 iterates, run with full rigor in a FRESH session. Do NOT change what each tool WRITES, only how many LLM turns invoke them.

title: Admit ci-security.json to the churn CHURN_ALLOWLIST (ensure_current aborts on it)
detail: CHURN_ALLOWLIST (shared/scripts/lib/churn_merge.py = DERIVED_MDS | events/test_results/triage) omits .shipwright/compliance/ci-security.json because it is a .json not a .md, so classify() marks it BLOCKING and ensure_current / resolve_churn_conflicts abort on it during iterate merges (hit while merging #374). Pure-classification fix: admit ci-security.json to the allowlist -- the regenerate path already covers it (regenerate_tracked_snapshots -> finalize_iterate._update_compliance returns every file under .shipwright/compliance/ via iterdir). Touches: churn_merge.py allowlist + module docstring + the test_churn_merge_doc_sync 1:1-registry test + docs/hooks-and-pipeline.md. resolve_churn_conflicts.py is cross_component -> integration coverage required (extend test_parallel_merge_cascade_integration.py). Full scope in memory project_finalization_bundling_plan.

title: Harden regenerate_tracked_snapshots to stage ci-security.json on a fresh-scan rewrite (forward parity with the rollback restore)
detail: Pre-existing (independent of #375): regenerate_tracked_snapshots stages only its out set (derived MDs + agent MDs + campaign statuses), never ci-security.json. If a fresh security.yml scan rewrites ci-security.json during an integrate regen, that rewrite is left modified-but-unstaged and the follow-up commit (staged-only) returns ok with a dirty local file. Benign: the pushed branch carries the --theirs mainline placeholder; only a local dirty line + a lost local refresh. #375 added ci-security.json to the regenerate-failure ROLLBACK restore set but not the forward staging set. Fix: record/stage ci-security.json when _update_compliance iterdir shows it changed, mirroring the rollback set. Full context in the #375 iterate spec + decision-drop.

title: Watch: flaky serial-drain push test (test_three_concurrent_iterates_drain_without_cascade) flaked once in CI
detail: The pre-existing real-git serial-drain test in shared/tests/test_parallel_merge_cascade_integration.py (#215) failed ONCE in CI on the #375 run: git push origin HEAD:main returned exit 1 (stderr swallowed by the test helper's check=True). It passed 6/6 locally including CI=true, and gh run rerun --failed went green on the SAME commit - a transient CI flake, not a code defect and unrelated to #375 (the changed rollback set only executes on regenerate-failure, which this test never triggers). NO fix needed now. If it recurs: surface the push stderr (drop check=True capture) and/or add a bounded push retry in the test helper. Low priority - watch only.

title: Plugin scope split: entry-point plugins (adopt/grade/run) global, 11 pipeline plugins project-scoped
detail: Scope the Shipwright marketplace correctly instead of enabling all ~14 plugins at user scope (they currently load /shipwright-* commands + hooks in every project, incl. non-Shipwright repos). SPLIT: keep GLOBAL only the plugins invoked in a not-yet-Shipwright dir -> shipwright-adopt, shipwright-grade, shipwright-run (codex stays global, untouched). Move the 11 pipeline plugins (project, design, plan, build, test, deploy, changelog, compliance, security, iterate, preview) to PROJECT scope; adopt + greenfield run WRITE that enablement block into the target repo's .claude/settings.json. LINCHPIN: installation (per-user cache) is separate from enablement (scope); the run phase-runner has no Skill tool -> reads phase SKILL.md from the cache path, so orchestration depends on install, not enablement. Externally reviewed 2026-07-16 (GPT-5.4 + Gemini, 2/2). 3 BLOCKERS: (B1) orchestrator must resolve cache paths explicitly, not via SessionStart-injected SHIPWRIGHT_PLUGIN_ROOT (only set when enabled) - verify via clean-env greenfield spike; (B2) fresh users may not have the 11 pipeline plugins INSTALLED after C1 -> install preflight in run, test with clean cache; (B3) already-adopted repos lose commands -> migration command. DECISIONS RESOLVED (owner, 2026-07-16): (D1) greenfield is run-only, shipwright-project stays project-scoped, global set = adopt/grade/run; (D2) solo use -> monorepo uses .claude/settings.local.json, tracked settings.json stays git-ignored (B4 moot). Plus JSONC-aware merge, secret-leak guard, marketplace-source conflict guard, min-version UX, prescriptive activation-lag messaging. Full spec + ACs: .shipwright/planning/iterate/iterate-2026-07-16-plugin-scope-split.md

title: Backfill iterate: write plugin/shared @FR tags (trg-01bb6cae 187 + trg-a57392a2 24) + thread config into TT5 _layer_coverage_regen enforcement

This resolves the extend-collector-test-root-scope FOLLOW-ON noted in trg-01bb6cae. Backfill scope: (1) auto-write the 187 high-confidence candidates (trg-01bb6cae) + review the 24 low-confidence proposals (trg-a57392a2); D-orphan validates the writes. (2) Thread configured_test_roots/configured_prune_dirs into shared/scripts/tools/verifiers/_layer_coverage_regen._build so the TT5 enforcing gates (removal + cross-layer) SEE plugin/shared coverage, handling the base-side config asymmetry (a base commit predating the config must fall back to default_test_roots when the key is absent at base). Without (2), once plugin tags exist, a plugin-dir-only-covered required layer reads covered in the RTM but MISSING/false-red in the enforcing gate. Source: campaign 2026-07-15-test-traceability-layers TT8 coverage-delta + the #387 doubt-review MEDIUM carry-forward.

title: FR taxonomy: capability-altitude FR minting (adopt) + Mint-vs-Fold gate (iterate) + FR-hygiene lint (compliance)
detail:
Root cause of badly-scoped, wrongly-numbered FRs in adopted specs: two FR-minting engines with incompatible philosophy. Fix across three plugins (full analysis + before/after mapping in the webui repo: Spec/design/2026-07-17-fr-taxonomy-regrouping.md).

(1) shipwright-adopt (feature_inferrer.py / artifact_writer.py): stop minting one FR per route. Group routes under the page/capability they serve (one FR per capability; routes become Interface bullets in the Description). Add an Area column + section-grouping (area from top route segment; fallback GEN). Keep the stable FR-01.NN ID scheme (IDs are referenced in append-only shipwright_events.jsonl the Mission view reads — never renumber). Strip HTTP verbs from the Name; dedup UI-page-FR vs backing-route-FR at the existing crawl-vs-AST url/route join.

(2) shipwright-iterate (path-a-feature.md / path-b-change.md / F5b): add an explicit MINT-vs-FOLD gate. FOLD (append AC to the existing FR) when the change completes/polishes/fixes/is Phase-N-of/extends an existing capability; MINT a new FR only for a genuinely new user/system-observable capability. Pin the number rule (new FR = next free FR-01.NN, deterministic max+1, filed into its Area section) to end guessed numbers + parallel-iterate collisions. Name fence: no GET/POST, snake_case symbols, ADR numbers or iterate slugs in the FR Name.

(3) shipwright-compliance: add an advisory FR-hygiene lint (new group), anti-ratchet/baseline style. Flag: altitude (Name carries a verb/symbol/ADR/slug), orphan-delta (Description says completes/modifies/replaces FR-x / Phase N of but is its own row -> fold candidate), missing Area, and UI-API duplicate FRs. Freeze existing violations; only new crossings block.

Cross-repo context: the webui spec.md is being sanitised in a parallel dedicated iterate (IDs kept stable, regroup+rename+fold, two tables: clean Requirements + a Fold/Alias map). These plugin fixes prevent the drift from recurring.

STEP 3 review-only DECISION: DISMISS. The ~6.8k FR-untagged monorepo tests are framework-internal (self-adopted to 15 coarse per-plugin FRs); bulk force-mapping to coarse buckets isn't meaningful traceability; targeted tagging still applies where a concrete FR fits. HEAD a419ce46: 6844 untagged/192 links/0 orphans; untagged_tests is informational. Rationale/scope/triggers in ADR 106 (planning/adr/106-step3-fr-unmapped-tests-accepted-state.md). GPT+Gemini endorsed DISMISS.

title: FR descriptions must be plain business language (adopt/project/iterate prompt rule)
detail:
Atomic business requirement: whenever the framework generates or updates a Functional Requirement, its description is plain business language (product-owner readable) - the WHAT plus behavioural guarantees, NOT implementation how (no file paths, ADR numbers, HTTP verbs, or code symbols; those go to architecture.md / the Source surface). Behavioural requirements are never dropped for plainness.

FR descriptions are LLM-agent prose (not code-assembled), so this is a PROMPT/reference change in THREE plugins - no string-assembly code changes:
- adopt: skills/adopt/references/step-b8-semantic-enrichment.md "Quality leitplanken". IMPORTANT: today it says "nuechtern, technical" / "describe what the code does" - that actively contradicts the rule and must be RECONCILED (replaced), not merely appended to.
- project: skills/project/references/spec-generation.md (Requirement Format + Writing Guidelines) - add the plain-business rule + the no-implementation-how fence to the Rupp/IREB guidance.
- iterate: skills/iterate/references/path-a-feature.md Step 2 ADD/MODIFY + path-b-change.md Step 2 - add the rule where the agent writes/updates the FR table-row description, plus 1-2 before/after examples.
- plan: nothing (maps FR IDs to sections only).
- compliance: OPTIONAL new spec_checks.py S-check to flag a description carrying a file path/ADR/verb/symbol (advisory, anti-ratchet) - enforcement only; none exists today.

Put the rule + examples in ONE shared place the three plugins reference (mirrors the FR-minting altitude rule). Companion to triage for FR-minting altitude (capability names, mint-vs-fold, area codes). Full spec written in the webui repo: Spec/design/2026-07-17-fr-business-language-plugin-spec.md. Out of scope: the one-time webui fold/rename/mapping (project-specific, already delivered in webui PR #287).
