title: Fold-aware traceability: resolve @covers folded FR IDs via FR-Fold-Map before orphan-flagging
detail: The test_links collector, D-orphan (_group_d_traceability) and backfill_scan read only survivor FR-table rows and do NOT resolve the spec FR-Fold-Map. A @covers tag on a FOLDED FR id is therefore classified fr_absent -> confirmed_orphan -> D-orphan MEDIUM (FAIL). Evidence: webui #287 (fr-taxonomy-regroup) folded 66 FRs into 29 capabilities; when the webui traceability retrofit #289 merged that spec, 22 tagged FRs were folded -> 419 orphans and D-orphan failed. Worked around by deterministically remapping folded->survivor via the fold-map. FIX: collector + D-orphan should resolve a tagged id through FR-Fold-Map to its survivor (identity for a survivor) before deciding orphanhood, so granular @covers tags survive a later taxonomy fold instead of hard-breaking. Until then tags must reference survivor ids only. Ref: webui .shipwright/compliance/test-traceability-coverage-delta.md follow-on #5.

title: WebUI: render resolved_from provenance for fold-resolved test links
detail: The traceability manifest can now file a test link against a surviving FR while the source tag names the folded id, recording it as resolved_from. A reader comparing the WebUI to the source will otherwise see a test linked to FR-01.28 whose code says covers(FR-01.44) with no explanation. When the WebUI surfaces per-FR test links it should render 'mapped from <folded id>' wherever resolved_from is set. Separate repo (shipwright-webui), so a separate iterate. Also worth confirming there: the manifest schema_version deliberately stays 2 (the new keys are optional and omitted when unused), so any vendored validator with additionalProperties:false should be checked before a fold-map repo is onboarded. Ref: shipwright#397.

title: Adopt + project: capability-level FR minting (route grouping + Area) and a decision on the greenfield/brownfield spec-table shape
detail:
Deferred remainder of trg-8e840ca0 / trg-44d23d63, split out of iterate-2026-07-18-fr-authoring-rules (which delivered the authoring RULES + the advisory Group I lint). This item is the STRUCTURAL half: it changes generated output and table schema, so it was kept out of a prompt-rules PR that had to stay reviewable and independently revertable.

(1) ADOPT MINTS ONE FR PER ROUTE - the root cause of endpoint-altitude FRs.
The real minting is NOT in feature_inferrer.py (that is the AST fallback only); it is generate_adoption_artifacts.py:312-365, which unions crawl+AST routes and assigns FR-01.{i} positionally. Required change: group routes under the page/capability they serve (the crawl's page URLs are the natural capability anchors); one FR per capability, with the routes becoming interface detail in architecture.md rather than sibling FRs. Add UI-vs-API dedup at the existing crawl-vs-AST url/route join, and strip HTTP verbs from the generated label. Keep the FR-01.NN id scheme - ids are referenced from the append-only shipwright_events.jsonl the Mission view reads, so they are never renumbered.
Files: plugins/shipwright-adopt/scripts/tools/generate_adoption_artifacts.py (716 LOC, grandfathered), scripts/lib/artifact_writer.py (695 LOC, grandfathered). Blast radius: test_features_additive_merge.py, test_artifact_writer.py, test_adopt_pipeline_subprocess.py, test_e2e_baseline_generator.py, test_traceability_layers.py, test_seed_traceability_baseline.py.

(2) AREA COLUMN - brownfield only, by design.
Adopt puts everything into one split (01-adopted), so the FR id can no longer express grouping and it must be restored as an Area column + section headers. Greenfield does NOT need this and must not get it: /shipwright-project already decomposes into splits and the split is encoded in the id (FR-04.02 = split 04). This is written up in shared/fr-authoring.md section 4 - keep it that way.

(3) DECIDE THE SPEC-TABLE SHAPE - AFFECTS shipwright-project, NOT ONLY ADOPT. This is a required DECISION with a deliverable either way; it is the only reason this item touches project at all, and it is easy to miss because points 1/2/4 are adopt-only.
Already done, do NOT redo: project's language + altitude work shipped in iterate-2026-07-18-fr-authoring-rules (spec-generation.md carries the plain-business rule, the no-implementation-how fence, and "one requirement, one capability"). What is open for project is ONLY the table shape.
The two generators disagree today:
  - /shipwright-project (greenfield): | ID | Requirement | Priority | Layers |   - one IREB "The system SHALL ..." sentence.
  - /shipwright-adopt  (brownfield):  | ID | Name | Priority | Description | Source | Layers |
Both are rendered by the WebUI and read by the compliance collectors; drift_parsers.parse_fr_table / rtm.collect_requirements tolerate the difference via an optional-group regex (ADR-031). traceability_layers._layers_index resolves its column BY HEADER NAME, so ADDING a column is safe - REMOVING or renaming one is not.
Pick one and record it:
  (a) CONVERGE - move greenfield to Name+Description so both generators emit one shape. This is a schema migration: it ripples into the two FR parsers, the RTM, traceability_layers and seed_traceability_baseline, and needs its own test re-baseline. Do it WITH point 1, not separately, so the parsers change once.
  (b) KEEP BOTH - a legitimate answer (the Rupp sentence is an IREB choice, not an accident), but then it must be written down as intentional in shared/fr-authoring.md, otherwise the divergence silently rots and the next person re-opens this same question.
Choosing (b) means project is never touched again by this item - that is fine, but it must be a decision, not a default. Group I reads both shapes header-driven, so it does not constrain the choice.

(4) STALE DOC (found while scouting, unrelated to the above).
plugins/shipwright-adopt/skills/adopt/references/feature-inference.md:109-110 states "AST-inferred features are used only when no crawl data exists". That contradicts shipped behaviour - generate_adoption_artifacts.py:342-360 unions AST-only routes into the crawl set (the fix pinned by test_features_additive_merge.py). Correct the doc.

(5) GROUP I ROBUSTNESS FOLLOW-UPS (from the review round; all low severity).
  - A spec that parses to zero rows is indistinguishable from an absent spec: both SKIP with "no FR rows found". A blind scanner therefore reads as "nothing to audit". Distinguish "no spec.md on disk" from "spec.md present but no FR table header recognised", and normalise header cells (strip bold/backticks) before the "id" test.
  - _column_map returns a bare None for both "not a header row" and "header I do not understand", so a second unrecognised table inside one section keeps the previous table's column mapping and can misread cells.
  - A raw unescaped pipe inside a Description cell shifts later columns (escaped pipes are handled). Cheap guard: skip a data row whose cell count exceeds the header's.

(6) PROMOTE I1/I2 FROM ADVISORY TO ANTI-RATCHET.
This iterate ships the advisory half deliberately: the requirement was that existing violations must not redden CI while a spec cleans up gradually, so I1-I3 never emit status=fail (that would feed AuditReport.any_fail and flip run_audit's exit code and the dashboard verdict). The ratchet half - freeze existing violations in a baseline, block only NEW crossings, mirroring shipwright_bloat_baseline.json - still needs a baseline file plus its own drift protection.

title: Requirements Catalog campaign: one catalog, one table shape, one discovery path
detail:
Umbrella anchor for the Requirements-Catalog campaign (S0-S8). Full spec + evidence + risk register: Spec/design/2026-07-18-requirements-catalog-campaign-SPEC.md (gitignored; read it before starting any sub-iterate).

PROBLEM. Requirements are scattered across planning documents and the folder determines the grouping. Five table shapes exist for the same table, five parsers read them, and nine independent discovery loops look for them with no shared helper. The divergence has already produced two wrong verdicts (ADR-031 predicted two stable formats; ADR-048 records a brownfield RTM reporting zero percent coverage). The FR gate validates only that a declared requirement id is non-empty, never that it exists. Requirement text has absorbed the changelog (417 lines for 15 requirements; one requirement carries about 145 lines of history).

OUTCOME. One catalog grouped by area in plain business language; planning documents keep the work detail and are linked from it. Each requirement records how it was established, including an honest assumed marker. Change history moves to a derived query over the append-only event log.

SHAPE. Target table is derived from the already-cleaned webui reference state (PR 287) plus a Layers column: ID | Area | Name | Priority | Description | Basis | Layers.

SEQUENCE. S0 existence gate, S1 test harness, S2 one discovery function, S3 namespace from the requirement id, S4 one header-driven parser, S5 converge the shape, S6 merge the catalog, S7 derived traceability view, S8 glossary plus targeted elicitation. S0-S4 change nothing observable and exist so S5-S6 become safe.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINT. The catalog must stay inside a split directory. A markdown file placed directly under planning/ matches none of the nine discovery walks, yielding zero requirements, which reads as pass or skip nearly everywhere while the spec-impact check simultaneously hard-fails every feature or change iterate. See section 6.1 of the spec before revisiting.

CROSS-REPO. The webui Mission campaign may start; it reads what this reshapes and PR 287 already built the target shape. After S3 merges, webui regenerates its manifest and RTM.

title: FR gate accepts requirement ids that do not exist (false green)
detail:
The finalization FR gate validates only that the declared requirement list is non-empty. is_non_empty_fr_list checks the strings and nothing else; whether a declared requirement actually exists is verified ONLY by detective check D2, which is medium severity, non-blocking, post-merge, and gated behind a spec-updated watermark.

Consequence: an iterate can declare an affected requirement that does not exist anywhere and the gate passes. This is why dangling references to non-existent requirement ids are present in the repo today.

This is a false green in the most load-bearing gate of the finalization chain, and it is independent of any table or catalog restructuring.

FIX. Validate declared requirement ids against the collected requirement set at write time in the event recorder and the finalizer, and hard-reject unknown ids with a message naming the offending id. The no-requirement change-type branch stays unchanged. Existing dangling references are reported, not auto-corrected.

Pulled forward as S0 of the Requirements-Catalog campaign (anchor trg-16d79da2) but independently shippable and worth doing first. Spec: Spec/design/2026-07-18-requirements-catalog-campaign-SPEC.md section 4 (S0).

reason: Superseded by the Requirements-Catalog campaign (anchor trg-16d79da2), which carries all six points forward: capability grouping and Area become S5/S6; the table-shape question resolves as CONVERGE onto the webui reference shape (PR 287) plus Layers; the stale feature-inference doc and the Group I robustness fixes fold into S5; the anti-ratchet promotion is deferred on purpose, since the catalog changes what would be frozen. Spec: Spec/design/2026-07-18-requirements-catalog-campaign-SPEC.md

title: Triage outbox: missing trailing newline concatenates two records, silently dropping one
detail:
A record in the triage outbox was written without a trailing newline, so the next writer appended onto the same physical line. The reader then fails to parse that line and SKIPS it with a warning, discarding BOTH records on it.

Observed today: one line held an append followed by a status record written by the WebUI. The status marked an item as dismissed with the note that it had been implemented. That dismissal never propagated, so the item still reads as open in the main log while the person who closed it believes it is done.

IMPACT. Silent loss on an append-only log. The warning goes to stderr and is easy to miss, and the reader treats a corrupt line as absent rather than as an error, so the loss is invisible in normal use. This is the same failure family as the earlier orphaned-status incident.

FIX DIRECTIONS. (1) Writers must guarantee the file ends with a newline before appending, rather than assuming the previous writer left one. (2) The reader should quarantine a corrupt line loudly instead of skipping it quietly, and should attempt to recover concatenated records by splitting on a record boundary. (3) A repair pass for lines already corrupted.

NOTE. The outbox file is untracked, so a corrupted line has no git history to recover from. Repair must preserve both records.

title: REQ-2 - Campaign: requirements catalog (S2-S8) - run AFTER REQ-1
detail:
THIRD of three. Order: REQ-0 (FR existence gate) -> REQ-1 (test harness) -> REQ-2 (this campaign). Do NOT start before REQ-1 is merged: every step here is verified against the harness REQ-1 builds, and without it "behaviour-preserving" is only an assertion.

Autonomous start, once REQ-1 is merged:
  /shipwright-iterate --campaign 2026-07-18-requirements-catalog

Campaign scaffolding: .shipwright/planning/iterate/campaigns/2026-07-18-requirements-catalog/ (read RUNBOOK/campaign.md first). Full spec, evidence and risk register: Spec/design/2026-07-18-requirements-catalog-campaign-SPEC.md (gitignored - read it before any sub-iterate).

WHAT IT FIXES. Requirements are scattered across planning documents and the folder determines the grouping. Five table shapes exist for one table, five parsers read them, nine independent discovery loops look for them with no shared helper. Two wrong verdicts already followed (ADR-031 assumed two stable formats; ADR-048 records a brownfield matrix reporting zero percent coverage).

OUTCOME. One catalog grouped by area in plain business language; planning documents keep the work detail and are linked from it. Each requirement records how it was established, including an honest assumed marker. Change history moves to a derived query over the append-only event log.

SUB-ITERATES. S2 one discovery function (nine call sites delegate). S3 namespace derived from the requirement id, not the file path (manifest schema bump). S4 one header-driven row parser; revise ADR-031. S5 converge the table shape. S6 merge the catalog. S7 traceability as a derived view. S8 glossary plus targeted elicitation. S2-S4 change nothing observable.

CRITICAL CONSTRAINT. The catalog must stay INSIDE a split directory. A markdown file directly under planning/ matches none of the nine discovery walks, yields zero requirements, and that reads as pass or skip nearly everywhere while the spec-impact check simultaneously hard-fails every iterate. See section 6.1 of the spec before revisiting.

title: REQ-1 - Iterate: requirements test harness (golden corpus) - run AFTER REQ-0, BEFORE REQ-2
detail:
SECOND of three. Order: REQ-0 (FR existence gate) -> REQ-1 (this) -> REQ-2 (campaign trg-1b764b2c). This is the safety net the whole catalog rebuild rests on; REQ-2 must not start before it is merged.

Autonomous start:
  /shipwright-iterate --type change "requirements test harness: golden corpus that freezes todays discovery and parser behaviour, including its known false verdicts"

WHY. Nine independent implementations walk .shipwright/planning/ looking for spec.md, with no shared helper, and five parsers read the resulting table. Steps S2-S4 of REQ-2 rewrite exactly that machinery and claim to be behaviour-preserving. Without a harness that claim cannot be checked, only asserted.

WHAT TO BUILD. A fixture corpus - greenfield multi-split, brownfield single-split, empty, malformed, mixed-shape - plus assertions pinning the CURRENT output of every discovery path and every parser for each fixture.

THE POINT MOST LIKELY TO BE GOT WRONG. The harness must freeze todays behaviour INCLUDING its bugs. Two known false verdicts must be recorded as current behaviour, each with a comment naming the later step that flips them: (1) a spec parsing to zero rows makes the traceability check SKIP rather than FAIL; (2) an empty manifest reads as green because "no requirements" is treated as "nothing to audit" nearly everywhere. Fixing them here would destroy the baseline the refactor is measured against - they are flipped deliberately in S4/S6, with the harness updated in the same commit.

Runs in CI, not marked slow. Spec: Spec/design/2026-07-18-requirements-catalog-campaign-SPEC.md section 4 (S1).

title: CI supply-chain guardrails + make an accepted risk actually stick (from webui #285 revert)
detail:
webui iterate-2026-07-18-unpin-actions-no-dependabot reverted PR #285
(SHA-pinned all first-party GitHub Actions + added Dependabot) and recorded the
posture as an ADR. Four FRAMEWORK gaps surfaced that let a reviewed change
contradict a recorded decision unnoticed. Ordered by leverage, not by cost:

1. PROPAGATE THE PRINCIPLE. Owner decision (2026-07-18): GitHub is repo host +
   Actions runner ONLY; no other GitHub-hosted proprietary service. Rationale is
   PORTABILITY, not cost - shipped CI templates
   (shared/templates/github-actions/*) and adopt scaffolding must not make
   adopters inherit Dependabot or any hosted service. NOTE: a parallel monorepo
   session (2026-07-18) independently reached the same conclusion via a
   cost/GHAS-availability argument; prefer the portability framing, which still
   holds where the service is free (webui is public, Dependabot is free there).

2. GAP 2 - ROOT CAUSE OF #285. An acceptance silences the TRIAGE item but NOT the
   code-scanning alerts. The repo keeps showing red for a consciously accepted
   risk, which trains the next person to "fix" it - exactly how #285 started.
   Accepting a risk must converge BOTH surfaces.

3. GAP 4 - CHEAPEST, HIGHEST LEVERAGE. classify_complexity.RISK_TAXONOMY has NO
   pattern for .github/workflows/** or .github/dependabot.yml. Changing the files
   that decide which third-party code runs with repository credentials fires zero
   risk flags. Verified TWICE: #285 recorded risk_flags: [] while running a full
   medium iterate (external plan review + code review + confidence calibration),
   and the revert reproduced risk_flags: [] on the same 7 files. Proposal: a
   touches_ci_supplychain flag, min_complexity small, enforcing mandatory review.

4. GAP 5. Nothing reads ADR Re-Review-Date - verified by grep across the
   compliance plugin and shared scripts. The ONLY expiry surface that reaches a
   human is the .trivyignore.yaml accepted-risk register rendered by the
   dashboard as "EXPIRED - re-review" (ci_security.py:163-201, :246-255). webui
   had to register a Semgrep/CI POSTURE decision in a TRIVY ignore file to get a
   due date - an acknowledged semantic stretch. Generalize it into a
   scanner-agnostic accepted-risk register.

5. GAP 3 - STRETCH, NOT COMMITTED. No audit check detects a change contradicting
   a recorded decision. Most expensive and least certain; listed so it is not
   lost, deliberately NOT promised - a triage item mixing four solvable things
   with one vague one tends to get dropped whole.

Evidence trail: webui .shipwright/planning/iterate/2026-07-18-unpin-actions-no-dependabot.md,
webui CLAUDE.md DO-NOT #25, webui server/src/test/ci-action-pinning-posture.test.ts.
launchPayload:
Framework follow-up to webui iterate-2026-07-18-unpin-actions-no-dependabot.
Start with item 3 (touches_ci_supplychain in classify_complexity.RISK_TAXONOMY) -
smallest diff, and it is the guard that would have caught #285. Then item 2
(acceptance must converge triage AND code-scanning), then item 1 (strip hosted
services from the shipped CI template), then item 4 (scanner-agnostic accepted-risk
register). Leave item 5 unless the others land cheaply. Read the webui ADR first so
this does not re-decide the posture - it is already decided and recorded.

title: Compliance: 5 open finding(s)
detail:

title: REQ-0 - Iterate: FR gate accepts requirement ids that do not exist (false green) - IN FLIGHT
detail:
FIRST of three. Order: REQ-0 (this) -> REQ-1 (test harness, trg-94337862) -> REQ-2 (campaign, trg-1b764b2c).

STATUS: code complete in worktree iterate/fr-existence-gate, NOT committed and NOT pushed. Remaining: bloat-baseline decision (record_event.py 820 -> 723), F0-F11, PR, watch to merged and green.

THE DEFECT. The finalization FR gate validates only that the declared requirement list is non-empty. Whether a declared requirement actually exists is verified ONLY by detective check D2, which is medium severity, non-blocking, post-merge, and gated behind a spec-updated watermark. An iterate can therefore declare a requirement id that exists nowhere and the gate passes - a false green in the most load-bearing gate of the finalization chain.

WHAT WAS BUILT. A new module lib/fr_gates.py holds BOTH FR gates behind one entry point run_fr_gates(), so a write path can no longer wire one gate and forget the other - the same bypass ADR-059 had to close. The pure predicate unknown_fr_ids lives in fr_classification (stdlib-only by contract). Both write paths - the CLI and the worktree finalize path - are wired.

GRADUATED ON PURPOSE. No planning directory means unverifiable, so it allows. Specs present but parsing to zero requirements allows too, but warns loudly - that is the blind-scanner case where silence reads as nothing to audit. Only an id outside a non-empty known set is a hard failure. Fail-open on unavailable is deliberately not fail-open on unknown, so a customer repo is never bricked by a gate it cannot satisfy.

VERIFIED. 4310 shared tests and 234 integration tests pass, lint clean, all touched files under their bloat baseline (record_event.py ratcheted DOWN from 820 to 723). A scan of 326 historical iterate events found ZERO ids the new gate would reject, so this is a pure tightening with no false-positive surface here.

reason: Renumbered for a clear running order. Superseded by REQ-0 (trg-d6c748b2) - same work, unchanged scope, now carrying its delivery status and the REQ-0/1/2 sequence marker so the next steps are self-explanatory on the board.

Renumbered and promoted to a real campaign. Superseded by REQ-2 (trg-1b764b2c), which is scaffolded at .shipwright/planning/iterate/campaigns/2026-07-18-requirements-catalog with seven sub-iterates and can be started autonomously once REQ-1 (trg-94337862) is merged.

Filed in error. Triage is a do-not-forget list, not a task list: work already in flight does not belong on it. This item was created for in-flight work that is being finished in the current session, so it never had anything to remind anyone of.

title: shipwright-webui: triage status writer must guarantee a trailing newline before appending
detail: The monorepo append boundary now guarantees the log ends with a newline before appending, and the reader recovers records that were concatenated onto one physical line (iterate-2026-07-18-outbox-newline-corruption). The webui has its own writer in a separate repo which still appends without that guard, so it can still produce the corrupt shape. Apply the same guard there, and mirror the record-boundary recovery in the webui reader. See shared/scripts/lib/jsonl_records.py for the contract.

reason: Moved to the shipwright-webui repo as trg-e16c7fea, where the writer and reader actually live. Not a monorepo defect: the monorepo halves were fixed in PR #399. Re-filed there with the ported contract and both file locations.

title: Three requirements-parser defects frozen by S1, fixed by campaign step S4
detail:
Three defects in the requirements table parsers, found while building the S1 golden corpus (campaign Requirements Catalog). All three are frozen as current behaviour in integration-tests/test_requirements_corpus_found_defects.py and are fixed by the SAME change - S4's single header-driven reader. Filed so they survive if S4 is descoped or resequenced.

FV-3: a requirements row carrying more columns than its header declares has a later column read as the requirement text, so the generated traceability matrix can display the wrong description for a requirement. Live in a shipped audit artifact.

FV-4: the requirements hygiene audit maps no columns at all when a table header names its first column FR instead of ID, dropping every row in that file. The four hygiene checks then report against zero rows, which reads as green.

FV-5: the same audit resets its column mapping at every heading, so a requirement row appearing under a later heading without a repeated header row is silently dropped.

Rationale and flip-step for each: integration-tests/requirements_corpus/frozen_bugs.py

title: Flaky idempotency test: dashboard render compared across a minute boundary
detail:
shared/tests/test_finalize_iterate.py::test_run_is_idempotent compares two generated dashboard renders for byte equality. Each render stamps a minute-resolution UTC timestamp into its banner, so when the two calls straddle a minute boundary the renders differ and the idempotency assertion fails.

Observed live on an unrelated PR: the two banners read 20:39 and 20:40 UTC, everything else identical. Nothing about the change under test was involved.

Effect: a time-dependent failure that reddens whichever pull request happens to run across the boundary, costing a re-run and eroding trust in a required check. The test also does not currently prove what it claims - a genuine idempotency regression would be indistinguishable from this flake.

Suggested direction: freeze the clock for the comparison, or compare with the banner line excluded, so the assertion measures idempotency rather than wall-clock alignment. A related banner-drift fix already exists in the events log helper but does not cover this path.

title: CI-Security 1/2: scanner-agnostic accepted-risk register that converges triage AND code-scanning
detail:
CI-Security 1 of 2. Splits items 4 + 2 out of anchor trg-9509c2e8 (webui
iterate-2026-07-18-unpin-actions-no-dependabot). Item 3 is DELIVERED as PR #401.

MERGED ON PURPOSE: item 4 builds the register, item 2 rewires acceptance onto it.
Done apart, item 2 would be built against the Trivy-shaped register and migrated
days later. One iterate.

GAP 4 - the only expiry surface that reaches a human is parse_accepted_risks over
.trivyignore.yaml, rendered by the dashboard as "EXPIRED - re-review"
(ci_security.py:163-201, rendered :246-255). Nothing reads an ADR Re-Review-Date
(verified by grep across the compliance plugin + shared scripts). webui therefore
had to register a SEMGREP / CI-POSTURE decision inside a TRIVY ignore file to get
a due date at all - an acknowledged semantic stretch. Generalize it into a
scanner-agnostic accepted-risk register.

GAP 2 - ROOT CAUSE OF #285. An acceptance silences the TRIAGE item but NOT the
code-scanning alerts. The repo keeps showing red for a consciously accepted risk,
which trains the next person to "fix" it - exactly how #285 started. Accepting a
risk must converge BOTH surfaces.

Do NOT re-decide the posture: GitHub-owned actions on mutable tags, third-party
SHA-pinned, no hosted updater. Portability, not cost. See webui ADR +
CLAUDE.md DO-NOT #25.

Suggested shape: register entry carries scanner + rule id + expiry + rationale ref;
one producer resolves it into (a) triage auto-dismiss and (b) a code-scanning
dismissal, so one acceptance quiets both. Expiry stays human-visible.

NOT IN SCOPE: item 5 (audit-level contradiction detection) stays deliberately
unpromised - a card mixing four solvable things with one vague one gets dropped
whole.

title: CI-Security 2/2: ship the action-pinning posture RULE to adopters (templates already correct)
detail:
CI-Security 2 of 2. This is what is LEFT of item 1 in anchor trg-9509c2e8 after
verification - most of it turned out to be already done.

VERIFIED ALREADY CORRECT (do not redo):
- shared/templates/github-actions/*.template already ship the decided asymmetry:
  GitHub-owned actions on mutable tags (actions/checkout@v4,
  github/codeql-action/...@v4, actions/upload-artifact@v4) and the one
  third-party action SHA-pinned (peter-evans/create-or-update-comment@71345be...).
- No dependabot config anywhere in shared/templates/ or the adopt plugin, so
  adopters already inherit no hosted updater.

WHAT IS ACTUALLY MISSING: the RULE is not shipped, only the current state. An
adopted repo gets correct workflows but nothing tells the next author why, so the
first well-meant "let us pin everything for supply-chain safety" silently reverses
the posture - which is precisely how webui #285 happened, in a repo that DID have
the state right.

Scope:
1. Carry the posture rule into shared/templates/claude-md-template.md so adopters
   inherit it the way webui carries CLAUDE.md DO-NOT #25.
2. A guard pinning the templates' asymmetry in BOTH directions (re-pinning a
   GitHub-owned action fails; unpinning the third-party action fails), mirroring
   webui server/src/test/ci-action-pinning-posture.test.ts. Without it the
   templates can drift back silently.

Do NOT re-decide the posture (portability, not cost - it holds even where the
hosted service is free). Independent of CI-Security 1/2 (trg-15a8e267): different
files, no shared machinery. Small.

reason: Superseded by the split. Item 3 DELIVERED as PR #401 (touches_ci_supplychain flag + non-dodgeable acknowledgement gate). Items 4+2 continue as trg-15a8e267 (CI-Security 1/2 - merged on purpose: 4 builds the register, 2 rewires acceptance onto it). Item 1 continues as trg-0ce59c05 (CI-Security 2/2 - reduced after verification: shipped templates already correct, only the RULE is missing). Item 5 left unpromised. Dismissed so the anchor stops double-counting its own children.

title: CI-Security 1b/2: converge an acceptance onto code-scanning + triage (GAP 2, root cause of #285)
detail:
CI-Security 1b of 2. Phase 2 of the split out of trg-15a8e267 (CI-Security 1/2,
which delivers the scanner-agnostic accepted-risk register itself). Numbering note:
2/2 is already taken by trg-0ce59c05 (adopter posture rule) - this is 1b, not 2.

WHY SPLIT, given the parent card said "MERGED ON PURPOSE": both external reviewers
(GPT-5.4 + Gemini 3.1 Pro) independently called the merged scope too large, and the
parent's stated reason for merging is satisfied by the split. Its worry was that
item 2 would be built against a Trivy-shaped register and migrated days later.
Phase 1 fixes the register shape first, so this card is built against the
generalized register from the start. The letter of the card is broken; its
rationale is not.

SCOPE - converge the second surface. Phase 1 makes an acceptance a recorded,
validated, time-bounded object with a both-directions drift gate and an expiry that
fails a required test. It does NOT quiet the code-scanning alerts, so the repo can
still show red for a consciously accepted risk. That is GAP 2, the root cause of
webui #285.

Build a single CLI (subcommands, one domain module) that resolves a register entry
into both surfaces: (a) dismiss the matching open triage items, (b) dismiss the
matching code-scanning alerts, with the dismissal comment derived from the register
entry. Read-only check mode first; mutation is a deliberate command, never an
automated CI sweeper - no scheduled job should hold the authority to mass-dismiss
security alerts.

VERIFIED PREMISE (do not re-litigate): code-scanning dismissals persist across
re-scans. 22 alerts dismissed 2026-06-27 carry most_recent_instance.commit_sha
7c303a8b (merged 2026-07-18) and are still dismissed. Note updated_at does NOT
advance on re-observation, so the naive probe reads as "never seen again" and
inverts the design - use most_recent_instance, not updated_at. This also corrects
the rationale recorded at decision_log.md:3319 and :3332 ("reappear each scan"),
which is factually wrong for dismissed alerts. What those ADRs actually rejected -
dismissal as a substitute for fixing - stays rejected.

CARRY THE UNRESOLVED REVIEW FINDINGS (from the Phase 1 review, all still open here):
- Define canonical per-target match keys before implementation. Rule id alone is not
  enough; loose matching dismisses unrelated alerts, strict matching creates
  permanent false drift. Reject ambiguous mappings rather than guessing.
- Verify the token can actually PATCH a code-scanning alert. Scope check says yes
  (public repo, repo scope, admin) but it was deliberately NOT proven by a test
  mutation, because the only honest test overwrites a real alert comment.
- Paginate the alert listing and verify repository identity against the checked-out
  repo before any mutation; an incomplete listing reads as "converged".
- Never dismiss an alert with no backing register entry.
- On expiry, decide explicitly how visibility is restored. Reopening only alerts
  this tool dismissed (identifiable by machine provenance in the comment) is the
  strong option; never reopen a human-dismissed alert.
- Register the new status tokens in triage_gc MACHINE_DISMISSERS / MACHINE_REASONS
  in the same diff - that pair is a decoupled SSoT and a new reason otherwise
  escapes the dismissed-pile GC.

NOT IN SCOPE: shipping the register and this convergence to adopted repos via
shared/templates. That is a third step, worth doing only once this is proven here,
and it is distinct from trg-0ce59c05 (which ships the action-pinning posture rule,
not the register).

reason: Delivered in PR #406 (iterate-2026-07-18-accepted-risk-alert-convergence): converge resolves github-dismissal entries onto live code-scanning, keyed (tool, rule, path), provenance-marked, expiry/removal reopens only its own dismissals. Triage half built against the local-scanner per-finding surface; the repo-wide gh-security aggregate is deliberately untouched.

title: Remaining shipwright_events.jsonl readers still drop concatenated records
detail:
iterate-2026-07-18-events-jsonl-record-boundary (PR #405) fixed the WRITERS (record_event.append_event/_idempotent, adopt event_seeder) and the canonical reader (lib/config.read_events). SIX read sites still use the pre-fix bare json.loads + skip-whole-line, so a concatenated line still discards BOTH records there.

Reachable in normal operation: shipwright_events.jsonl carries merge=union, and union merge is line-based, so an ordinary merge of two worktrees joins an unterminated blob last line to the next side first line. No write-time lock can guard that.

TWO TIERS, deliberately not fixed together with #405 to keep that scope honest:
  A) NO import barrier - trivially fixable, same one-line delegation as config.read_events:
     - shared/scripts/lib/events_log.py:172-174 (latest_event_dt)
     - shared/scripts/lib/events_log.py:219-221 (finalized_run_ids)
     - shared/scripts/tools/verifiers/common.py:156-158
  B) ADR-045 sys.modules[lib] barrier - the plugin ships its own scripts/lib, so "from lib import jsonl_records" resolves THERE and raises ImportError (verified empirically in PR #405 for the adopt plugin). Needs the same documented-duplicate treatment, or a real fix to the shared-import story:
     - plugins/shipwright-compliance/scripts/lib/collectors/change_history.py:126-128
     - plugins/shipwright-compliance/scripts/audit/group_b.py:83-85 (silent continue, no warning)
     - plugins/shipwright-compliance/scripts/audit/group_d.py:74-76

Why it matters: these feed the compliance change-history, the audit groups and the F11 verifiers - a dropped work_completed makes a step that happened read as one that never did.

Reference implementation: lib/config.read_events in PR #405, and the regression home shared/tests/test_events_newline_integrity.py.

title: phase_timings sidecar append has no newline guard (lower risk: no union merge)
detail:
shared/scripts/lib/iterate_phase_groups.py:115 appends to .shipwright/agent_docs/iterates/<run_id>.phase_timings.jsonl with a bare open(path,"a") and no ends_without_newline probe - the same unguarded-append shape fixed for triage.jsonl (#399) and shipwright_events.jsonl (#405).

DELIBERATELY FILED SEPARATELY AND AT LOW SEVERITY: this sidecar is GITIGNORED, so unlike the other two logs it carries NO merge=union driver. The dominant vector - an ordinary git merge concatenating across an unterminated blob - does not apply. What remains is only a genuinely interrupted write, which is far rarer. Bundling it with the events-reader work would have implied a shared urgency that the evidence does not support.

Fix is a two-line change (probe + conditional separator) reusing lib/jsonl_records.ends_without_newline; the module is in shared/scripts/lib, so there is no ADR-045 import barrier. Also worth checking whether its reader recovers or skips.

title: Event-log readers: remaining sites still parse one record per physical line
detail: iterate-2026-07-19-events-record-boundary-readers converted 11 read sites to the shared record-boundary SSoT (lib/jsonl_records), including the F11 check_events_has_commit oracle whose failure mode was INVERTED (a concatenated line made F11 fail finalization for a correctly-recorded run). FIVE sites remain on the pre-fix one-record-per-line idiom, in the adopt, grade and run plugins plus two shared modules. Same defect class, same one-line remedy. Exact locations are enumerated in the iterate spec under 'Out of scope'. Lowest priority of the five reads a different file that self-documents as telemetry, not authority. Note validate_event_log.py is deliberately excluded - per-line strictness is its contract. Also filed there: churn_merge.validate_events_text can report a false failure when a run's work_completed is the second record on a concatenated line (fails closed, no data loss).
